## PAGE 06660: Working with model elements

- page_id: `243968116`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968116/Working+with+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:50.645+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Containment Tree

29726528

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Containment Tree

29726530

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Containment Tree

29726529

#### EXCERPT: Containment Tree

INLINE

The chapter outlines various methods of working with your model's elements. You will find detailed instructions on creating and defining model elements, default property values, extracting, defining hyperlinks, etc. You will also learn how the modeling tool shortcut menus, toolbar, and browser enable you to easily edit model elements.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']true

To create elements in the Containment tree

1. In the Containment Tree, right-click the element owner where you want to create a new model element.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Create Element .
3. A list with available elements appears. Select the element you need or start typing an element name in the Search box to find it faster.
4. Type the name of the element.

You can also create an element directly on the diagram pane. In this case, a symbol of the element and the element in the diagram owner is created.

To create elements on the diagram pane

1. Open the diagram or create a new diagram where you want to create a new model element.
2. From the diagram palette, select an element and drag it on the diagram pane. The element symbol is created.
3. Click the symbol and type the element name.

#### TIP: Containment Tree

Containment Tree

Please note, the new element is created in the Model Browser as well.

29726527

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965775 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06662: Working with model elements in the Model Browser

- page_id: `243965775`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965775/Working+with+model+elements+in+the+Model+Browser
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:21:49.829+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Model Browser
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1426024704

1426024708

1426024707

INLINE

In the Model Browser, you can do the various actions with model elements, such as rename, cut, copy, delete, and others. These actions are available from the particular model element shortcut menu. See the list of concrete actions in the following table.

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Rename | Switches the element name into the edit mode. You can also press F2 for this. |
| Copy | Copies the selected element. |
| Copy URL | Copies a model element URL to a clipboard. |
| Copy Element Hyperlink | Copies the hyperlink of the selected element. |
| Cut | Cuts the selected element. |
| Paste | Pastes the copied element. |
| Delete | Deletes the selected element. |

To copy/cut and paste the selected model element between different projects

1. Select the model element in the Model Browser. 
 2. From the model element shortcut menu, select **Copy** or **Cut**. 
 3. Open the project to which you want to paste the selected element(s). 
 4. Select the container, where you wish to paste the element(s). 
 5. From the container's shortcut menu, select **Paste**.

To change the location of an element in the Model Browser

1. Select the element in the Model Browser. 
 2. Drag it to the new location (possible owner).

1426024703

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965882 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06664: Working with multiple projects

- page_id: `243965882`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965882/Working+with+multiple+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:48.106+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Creating projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428492948

1428492951

1428492950

INLINE

You may need to manage several projects at the same time, and the modeling tool allows you to do that easily. All open projects are held in separate workspaces. Different active projects may exchange data. Entities from one project can be copied or moved to another.

To switch between open projects, do one of the following

- From File menu, select Switch Projects and then click the name of the project you want to switch to.
- On the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='switch_projects_button.png'] and then select the name of the project you want to switch to. [ATTACHMENT filename='switching_between_projects.png']

To close all open projects

- Select Close All Projects from the File menu. The Question message appears.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Choose the way your projects will be closed:

| Yes | The project you are currently closing will be saved (its name appears in the question). The dialog is displayed again when the next project closes. |
| --- | --- |
| Yes To All | Save all projects without prompting. The Save dialog will not appear for each open project. |
| No | Project you are currently closing will not be saved. The dialog is displayed again when the next project closes. |
| No To All | All the projects will be closed without saving or further prompting. |
| Cancel | Cancel saving projects. |

To exchange model elements between open projects

- Use the Cut , Copy , and Paste commands from the Edit menu or the selected element's shortcut menu, or the appropriate shortcut keys: Ctrl + X, Ctrl + C, Ctrl + V.

1428492947

**Rrelated pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Properties dialog' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563168 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06665: Working with NCV-5 typical table

- page_id: `249563168`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563168/Working+with+NCV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:04.459+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

628010590

628010604

628010588

**On this page**

628010594

##### Creating NCV-5 typical table

The context of the NCV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating NCV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the NCV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the NCV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the NCV-5 typical table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a NCV-5 typical table

To create an empty NCV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a NCV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > NCV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more NCV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a NCV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 typical table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

628015084

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e954108b-dbf8-4937-9e5c-1ed1e64aa9d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628010590</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="721f3385-b256-42eb-b272-71053c94868d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628010604</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66b4cae7-621e-47b7-9fb0-442ce64536eb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628010588</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4d42fb2-d091-4e3a-82fd-3ce686eebd79" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4082f5fe-c38c-456a-8a89-f72ba533562b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628010594</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating NCV-5 typical table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the NCV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating NCV-1 diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[NCV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The content of the NCV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the NCV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.</p><p>Resources are related to Capabilities using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the table cell is filled.</p><p>Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.</span> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-2-11_14-17-4.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.</p><p>There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a NCV-5 typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a NCV-5 typical</span> table</h4><p>To create an empty NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a NCV-5 package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>NCV-5</strong> <strong>Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>NCV-5</strong> <strong>Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><p>An<em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6cdec1b-3c94-432e-961c-856ebfd8a021"><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.</li><li>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.</li><li>versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.</li><li>Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Removing Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more NCV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Resources from a NCV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Resources from a NCV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the NCV-5 typical table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cd85468-a942-4243-ae40-7864a6e589ac"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">628015084</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563284 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06666: Working with NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249563284`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563284/Working+with+NSOV-3+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:13:21.861+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF > NATO Service-Oriented Viewpoint > NSOV-3 Services to Operational Activities Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **NSOV-3****Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>NSOV-3 </strong><strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a NSOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" /></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564147 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06667: Working with Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `249564147`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564147/Working+with+Operational+Activities+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:38.121+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282484386

1282484407

1282484397

The Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****shows the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Activities and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create an Operational Activities to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Activities).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Operational Activities maps to Capabilities using the Maps To Capability relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Activity to a Capability

To map an Operational Activity to a Capability

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282484385

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

Activities and the Columns are Capabilities.</p><p>To create an Operational <span>Activities to Capabilities Mapping</span> Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Capabilities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564025 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06668: Working with Operational Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249564025`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564025/Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:32.183+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Operational Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Operational Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Operational Connectivity and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

An Operational Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges

Before creating Operational Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Operational Information, Resource Information, or Resource). The you can start creating Operational Exchanges.

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Operational Performer and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Operational Exchange . Click another Operational Performer to connect. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Operational Exchange** and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

##### Realizing Operational Exchanges on Associations

When you have Operational Performers connected with an Association, there are two ways to create operational exchanges:

- Opening Exchange Manager.
- Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.

###### Opening the Exchange Manager

To create new Operational Exchange on the Association

1. Click the Association between Operational Performers.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Operational Exchange. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_op_exchange.png']
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
5. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog.

To open an **Operational Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.
2. In the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Operational Exchange Manager dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Operational Exchanges on the Association.

###### Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association

You can create the Operational Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the wizard, specify the needed information.

In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="707fdfcb-d53e-4825-bd53-df7c62c87aa2" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p>To create an Operational Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Connectivity and do one of the following:</span><ul><li>From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Operational Connectivity</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Operational Connectivity </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Operational Performer and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_op_performer_containment.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_op_performer.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p>An Operational Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and <span>Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</span></p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><h3>Creating Operational Exchanges</h3><p>Before creating Operational Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Information</span>, Resource Information, or Resource). The you can start creating Operational Exchanges.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<span><br /></span><ul><li><span>On the diagram pane, select an Operational Performer and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Operational Exchange</span>.<span> Click another Operational Performer to connect.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li><span> In the diagram palette, c</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">lick <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.</span><span> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange_palette.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span><br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16de18c6-2f39-40a4-a2f2-b9b40b154023"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. (<em>Optional</em>)</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4c4befc-85e4-439e-84bb-0c6c8f57299d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Realizing Operational Exchanges on Associations</h3><p>When you have Operational Performers connected with an Association, there are two ways to create operational exchanges:</p><ul><li>Opening Exchange Manager.</li><li>Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.</li></ul><h4>Opening the Exchange Manager</h4><p>To create new Operational Exchange on the Association</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the Association between Operational Performers.</li><li>In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Operational Exchange. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_op_exchange.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1810a3e-531e-49fa-b3c9-57d324bb8ed3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. (<em>Optional</em>)</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_exchange_on_association.png" /></ac:image></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open an <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">an Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li>In the smart manipulator, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange Manager"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange_manager.png" /></ac:image></span>.<br />The <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog opens.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="op_exchange_manager.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Operational Exchanges on the Association.</p><h4>Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association</h4><p>You can create the Operational Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_operational_exchange_1_1.png" /></ac:image></p><p>In the wizard, specify the needed information. </p><p>In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564055 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06669: Working with Operational Connectivity Table

- page_id: `249564055`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564055/Working+with+Operational+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:33.111+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An **Operational Connectivity Table** addresses operational exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add new or existing Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Operational Exchanges

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read-only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An <strong>Operational Connectivity Table</strong> addresses operational exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchange to fill in an Operational Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Operational Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select Operational Exchanges by clicking the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sending Operational Agent, Receiving Operational Agent, and Implements cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564065 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06670: Working with Operational Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `249564065`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564065/Working+with+Operational+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:33.986+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Operational Performers.
2. Connect them using the Associations relationship.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.

An Operational Constraints Definition view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] diagram.

##### Creating an Operational Exchange in Operational Constraints Definition diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Constraints Definition diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of Operational Performers and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating an Operational Constraints Definition diagram</h3><p>To create an Operational Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Performers.</li><li>Connect them using the Associations relationship.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Association.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>An Operational Constraints Definition view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Internal Connectivity]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><h3>Creating an Operational Exchange in Operational Constraints Definition diagram</h3><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized </strong><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Operational Exchange</strong></span> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of Operational Performers and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png" /></ac:image></span>. </li><li> <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_exchange.png" /></ac:image></span> and on the diagram pane, connect Operational Performers.</span> </span></li></ul><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564062 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06671: Working with Operational Constraints table

- page_id: `249564062`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564062/Working+with+Operational+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:06.127+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] and in the Select Subject Of Operational Constraint dialog, select constrained Operational Elements (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Operational Information, Operational Exchange).
3. In the **Rule** **Specification**cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the **Rule Kind** from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Operational Constraint for one constraint.

To create an Operational Constraints table by adding a new Operational Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In Select Operational Constraint dialog, select Operational Constraints. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564139 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06672: Working with Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249564139`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564139/Working+with+Operational+High+Level+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:07.796+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.

A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.

To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram

1. Create Concept Roles.
2. Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).
3. Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="wiki-content"><p>An Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram is based on the UML Composite Structure Diagram.</p><p>A Concept Role here is a property for a High-Level Operational Concept element. It represents a role that an element (the type of this property) is playing in this High-Level Operational Concept. According to the UAF specification Concept Role may represent (to have a type set as) an Operational Node, any of the Resources (System, Systems Node, etc.) or Location. The difference among Concept Role, Operational Node Concept Role and other Roles is the type or in other words, the represented element of the Concept Role.</p><p>To create an Operational High Level Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Concept Roles.</li><li>Specify the types of the Concept Roles (make sure you have Resource Taxonomy and Operational Taxonomy diagrams completed).</li><li>Associate Concept Roles using Arbitrary Relationships.</li></ol></div>
````

## PAGE 06673: Working with Operational Information diagram

- page_id: `249564073`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564073/Working+with+Operational+Information+diagram

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564041 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06674: Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249564041`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564041/Working+with+Operational+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:32.666+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

3

##### Creating Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

The owner of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram must be an Operational Performer.

To create an Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Operational Roles.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors .
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors .

Create the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Composite Structure element using any method that is most suitable for you:

- Right-click the Composite element in a Structure Diagram and Create Diagram.
- Right-click the Composite element in the Containment Tree and Create Diagram.
- Select the Internal Connectivity Diagram from the Smart Manipulator.

The diagram creation wizard is displayed, showing the hierarchical list of part properties owned by the selected composite element. All the elements or any subset can be included in the diagram.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges in Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector between a pair of Operational Roles, and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector .
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Operational Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Operational Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Operational Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564077 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06675: Working with Operational Parametric diagram

- page_id: `249564077`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564077/Working+with+Operational+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:35.256+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Parameters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

495805059

495805061

495805060

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED']to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED']and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Performer context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

495805058

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564150 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06676: Working with Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `249564150`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564150/Working+with+Operational+Performers+to+Capabilities+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:38.507+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Op-Tr)****displays the mapping between the capabilities required and the operational performers that those capabilities support.

##### Creating Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

The Rows of this matrix are Operational Performers, Operational Architecturesand their specializations, andthe Columns areCapabilities.

To create the Operational Performers to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Operational Performers, Operational Architectures).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] Refresh button.

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

##### Mapping an Operational Performer to a Capability

Operational Performers and Operational Architectures map to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To map Operational Performers to a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

##### Removing the relation from the matrix

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564085 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06677: Working with Operational Process Flow diagram

- page_id: `249564085`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564085/Working+with+Operational+Process+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-11-06T10:26:08.503+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Processes diagram' space=''] must be created first.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram for Operational Activity flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Performer or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Operational Activity Actions or drop Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Operational Process Flow diagram with the top element -*Distress Signal Handling*as a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Operational Activity Action or Operational Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Operational Process Flow diagram for an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create an Operational Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of an Operational Activity Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Operational Activity Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Operational Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Performer or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new OperationalProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Operational Activity Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Performer and Operational Activity (the behavior of the Operational Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Operational Activity Action.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Swimlanes" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564083 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06678: Working with Operational Processes diagram

- page_id: `249564083`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564083/Working+with+Operational+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:06.823+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies

1. Create Operational Activities.
2. Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an Operational Processes diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Activities.</li><li>Create Operational Performers or reuse them from Operational Taxonomy view (recommended).</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Operational Performers and Operational Activities.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Operational Activities if necessary.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 06679: Working with Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

- page_id: `249564057`

- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:05.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add anexisting Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table

1. In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click **+** button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A role-based table represents exchanges between Operational Roles (the usages of Operational Agents). You can add <span class="confluence-link">an </span><span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors to fill in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connectors in the Operational Role-based Connectivity Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Operational Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>Double-click Operational Exchange/Operational Connector from the list to add them in the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Operational Exchange/Operational Connector, and click <strong>+</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges/Operational Connectors are added to the table.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564092 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06680: Working with Operational Sequences diagram

- page_id: `249564092`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564092/Working+with+Operational+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:36.690+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Sequences
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Exchange in the Operational Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Operational Roles and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and on the diagram palette, select the Operational Message.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Operational Exchanges data.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']

Exchange" ac:alt="New Operational Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_operational_exchange.png" /></ac:image></span></span>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables to analyze and manage the Operational
