<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564103 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06681: Working with Operational Structure diagram

- page_id: `249564103`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564103/Working+with+Operational+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:37.452+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Operational Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Operational Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Operational Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Operational Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.select_aggregation.png']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Relationship Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Operational Performers were related using the Operational Association relationship, select the **Operational Association** also.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='op.str.display_related_nested.png']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564127 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06682: Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249564127`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564127/Working+with+Operational+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:31.497+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

911370602

911370604

478104274

**On this page**

911370603

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Operational Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Operational Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Operational Taxonomy**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Operational Taxonomy**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Operational Performer element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an Operational Performer in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Operational Performer and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Operational Performer in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Operational Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Operational Performer and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_op_performer.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Operational Performer (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connect the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Make sure that **All** is chosen for **Properties**.
3. Search for **Is Abstract** and set it to *true*.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

- An Operational Taxonomy view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.
- The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''] .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564141 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06683: Working with Operational Taxonomy table

- page_id: `249564141`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564141/Working+with+Operational+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:07.908+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Operational > Operational Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an Operational Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button and select Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, or Operational Performer. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Element dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p> To create an Operational Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Known Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, or Operational Performer. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562936 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06684: Working with OV-2 diagram

- page_id: `249562936`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562936/Working+with+OV-2+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:58.308+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-2 Operational Node Relationship Description
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

414455593

414455596

414455592

**On this page**

414455595

##### Creating OV-2 diagram

To create an OV-2 diagram

1. Create Operational Architectures.
2. Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational Performers.
3. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connector.
4. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connector.

##### Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space=''] . To open the wizard, do either:
  - Select a Operational Connector and on the smart manipulator, click the New Operational Exchange button.
  - On the Operational Exchanges toolbar in the diagram palette, click the Operational Exchange button and then click the Operational Connector.
2. Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.
3. Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.

An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.

The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operational Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.

Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the OV-5 diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3da90bc1-80c5-4034-a9d7-4667bdea132d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455593</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a14cf15-7ecd-439b-b8f2-7d04b43d3f53"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455596</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="da8c3f64-4014-4d9d-ada5-7ba55fc4899a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455592</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4d2e7ae4-38ea-4e93-91fd-966e796b942b" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7b70ff-1287-4ca5-b5fa-a10e962bb602"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">414455595</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating OV-2 diagram</h3><p>To create an OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Operational Architectures.</li><li>Model internal structures of created context elements using Operational Roles typed by Operational  Performers.</li><li>Associate Operational  Roles using Operational Connector.</li><li>Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connector.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Creating Operational Exchanges in OV-2 diagram</h3><p>To create an Operational Exchange in the OV-2 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></strong>. To open the wizard, do either:<ul><li>Select a Operational Connector and on the smart manipulator, click the New Operational Exchange button.</li><li>On the <strong>Operational Exchanges</strong> toolbar in the diagram palette, click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> button and then click the Operational Connector.</li></ul></li><li>Specify the Operational Exchange item either by choosing an existing Operational Exchange or creating a new one.</li><li>Specify the direction of the Operational Exchange.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>An OV-2 view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Operational Performers, Physical Location requirements, and Services provided or requested by Operational Performers.</p><p>The structure of each Operational Performer can be modeled using the Operational Node Internal Relationship Description diagram or in a special structure compartment of this element shape.</p><p>Once the OV-2 diagram has been completed, you can proceed creating the OV-5 diagram.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562943 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06685: Working with OV-3 table

- page_id: `249562943`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562943/Working+with+OV-3+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:26.922+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-3 Operational Information Exchange Matrix
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-3 table

1. Add existing Operational Exchanges.
2. Click the Add Existing button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connector. In case a Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.

- You need to use an OV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.
- You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-3 table</p><hr /><ol><li>Add existing Operational Exchanges.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button to select Operational Exchanges or Operational Connector. In case a Operational Connector has been selected, all Operational Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="24c45741-9173-4ab5-b2f7-90d1adf18b9d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You need to use an OV-2 to create or modify Operational Exchanges.</li><li>You can remove the rows of Operational Exchanges from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to open the shortcut menu.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562948 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06686: Working with OV-4 diagram

- page_id: `249562948`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562948/Working+with+OV-4+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:27.082+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-4 diagram

1. Create Organizations and Posts.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.
3. Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.
4. Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.

You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.

You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.

You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an OV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.

Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before SV-1 view.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-4 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations and Posts.</li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li><li>Create Actual Organizations and Actual Posts.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational resources with Actual Resource Relationship.</li></ol><p>You can also display Competencies of the Organizational Resources.</p><p>You can use the Commands relationship to display the Operational Resource that is in charge of others.</p><p>You can display Persons and Actual Persons in an OV-4. Actual Persons can fill in Actual Posts. You can display this relationship using the Fills Post dependency.</p><p>Organizations Resources are the resources and in general they could be modeled just before SV-1 view.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562963 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06687: Working with OV-6a table

- page_id: `249562963`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562963/Working+with+OV-6a+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:27.666+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-6a Operational Rules Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following

- You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.
- You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.
- Right-click on a cell to see more menus.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-6a table, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><ul><li>Add a new Operational Constraint by clicking the <strong>Create New Operational Constraint</strong> button and select constrained Operational Element (Operational Performer, Operational Activity, Information Element, Operational Exchange, and Mission). Fill in the specification cell with an expression that can be written in natural or technical language (for example English or OCL).</li><li>Add Existing Operational Constraints by clicking the<strong> Add Existing Operational Constraint</strong> button and select Operational Constraints from the model.</li></ul></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="301160f6-1ced-453f-b022-3d77f727858d"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can add or edit constrained elements in every row in the table.</li><li>You can also remove Rows (Operational Constraints) from the model or table, order, or export them to a CSV or HTML format.</li><li>Right-click on a cell to see more menus.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562977 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06688: Working with OV-7 diagram

- page_id: `249562977`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562977/Working+with+OV-7+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:27.982+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Operational Viewpoint > MODAF. OV-7 Information Model
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create an OV-7 diagram

1. Create Entity Items.
2. Add Properties.
3. Draw Associations.

Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Details relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create an OV-7 diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Entity Items.</li><li>Add Properties.</li><li>Draw Associations.</li></ol><p>Entities can be linked with Exchange Elements. To relate these UAF Elements, use Details relationship.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563697 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06689: Working with P1 Resource Types Table

- page_id: `249563697`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563697/Working+with+P1+Resource+Types+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:02.201+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Physical Resource viewpoint > P1 Resource Types
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Resource Types****Table (P1)**provides all the available resource types.

To add a new element in the Resource Types table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose from the following:
  - Capability Configuration
  - Known Resource
  - Natural Resource
  - Organization
  - Person
  - Post
  - Project
  - Resource Architecture
  - Resource Mitigation
  - Resource Service
  - Responsibility
  - Security Enclave
  - Software
  - System
  - Technology A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.

To add an existing element in the Resource Types table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Resource Performer dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Capability Configuration, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Resource Architecture, Resource Mitigation, Resource Service, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, Technology).
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added Resource Performer.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> <strong>Resource Types</strong></span><strong><span> </span>Table (P1)</strong><span> </span>provides all the available resource types.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new element in the Resource Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose from the following:<ul><li>Capability Configuration</li><li>Known Resource</li><li>Natural Resource</li><li>Organization</li><li>Person</li><li>Post</li><li>Project</li><li>Resource Architecture</li><li>Resource Mitigation</li><li>Resource Service</li><li>Responsibility</li><li>Security Enclave</li><li>Software</li><li>System</li><li>Technology<br />A new row is added.</li></ul></li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing element in the Resource Types table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Select Resource Performer </strong>dialog, choose the desired operational concept (Capability Configuration, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Organization, Person, Post, Project, Resource Architecture, Resource Mitigation, Resource Service, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, Technology).</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the added Resource Performer.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565426 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 06690: Working with Parametric diagram

- page_id: `249565426`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565426/Working+with+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:57:18.561+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

492938369

492938371

492938370

In all Parametric diagrams you can:

- .
- . To perform this step, the should be installed.

defineTo define the parametric formula structure

1. On a diagram, display or create Measurement properties (Measurements will be used as arguments or result of calculation). The owner of the Measurements will be the same as for the diagram.
2. Define types for the measurements (e.g. Primitive types like Integer, Real.)
3. Create the Constraint Property which type should be Constraint Block (create new or select existing).
4. Once type is set, define the calculation formula in the curly brackets in the diagram.
5. Create the Constraint Parameters for each member of the formula in the Constraint Property.
6. Use Binding Connector to connect Measurements to the proper Constraint Parameter.

For more information about Parametric diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='']

When the formula structure is defined, you can initiate the simulation.

simulateTo simulate parametrics

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space=''] the Parametric diagram owner (e.g. Operational Performer).
2. After the instances are created, define the slot values of the Measurements representing arguments in the formula.
3. Select the created instance and from the shortcut menu, select Simulation > Run . The simulation process is initiated.

If you want to simulate more than one instance, you can do that in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space=''].

The predefined [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space=''] can be applied to the UAF elements.

492938367

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instantiating structures' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard in UAF' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance table' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Modeling parametric constraints' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966148 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06691: Working with partially loaded projects

- page_id: `243966148`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966148/Working+with+partially+loaded+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:54.154+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Managing used projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418017743

418017745

418017744

To increase modeling efficiency when working with very large projects, you can work with partially loaded used projects. This may help decrease memory consumption.

Here, “large” is tens of thousands of classes and other complex elements. If counting all small elements, such as properties, methods, method parameters, several hundreds of thousands of elements is considered a large project. Note that diagrams are large elements. A project consisting of 1000 or more complex diagrams should be considered large.

You can unload a particular used project at any time when working with a large partitioned project.

To unload a used project from a project

- Right-click the selected used project, and select Project Usages > Unload .

Not loaded (or unloaded) used projects can be loaded in a project anytime. For more information about managing used projects, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space=''].

To load a used project in a project

- From the unloaded used project's shortcut menu, select Used Projects and then Load Used Project.

When the used project is neither loaded nor unloaded, there can be some model elements left from the used project. These elements are not editable and are characterized by a small M letter in the upper left corner of their icon (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Notation of unloaded used project elements (fragment of Containment tree)

These are the so-called “proxy” elements of the original elements from the used project. Instead of the original model elements, the proxy is a lightweight surrogate that carries only the name and kind of the model element information. These proxies are left in place of the original used project elements and are dedicated to maintaining model integrity (ensuring that there are no dangling ends of relationships, types of properties do not disappear, etc.) while the used project is not accessible or not loaded.

There are 4 used project loading modes:

- Always load (default). In this mode, used projects are always loaded when you open the project. They can be unloaded if necessary.
- Autoload . A used project is not loaded while the project using it is loaded. However, our modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and tries to guess when you might want to use the model piece from the unloaded used project. For example, if you search and find usages/dependencies, reports, metrics, transformations, or code engineering actions with a scope that touches the unloaded used project, our modeling tool will load that used project.
- Autoload with prompt . This mode differs from the **Autoload** mode in that our modeling tool asks you before loading the used project.
- Manual load . A used project is not loaded while the project using it is loaded. It can be loaded using the **Load** used project command (described above).

To change the used project loading mode

1. From the Options menu, select Used Projects. The Used Projects dialog opens.
2. In the used project's tree, select a used project.
3. In the Used Project Load Mode area, select the appropriate loading mode.

Always load

Occasionally used projects should be set in **Autoload** mode (or **Autoload with prompt** to have more control on loading behavior). Very rarely used projects can be set in the **Manual load** mode. Another frequent case where used projects can be set to **Manual load** mode occurs when the used projects represent a software library that is not expected to change.

418017742

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maintaining decomposed model integrity' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dd8e73c-ecc0-49be-a02f-6928d92e9d7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418017743</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="80c78bf6-b98d-49a6-b504-f9e139da3fba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418017745</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c2aff252-64c3-45bd-81cf-ff85da62b9e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418017744</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="5ce4da86-fee1-402c-9db2-3126cc644d89">To increase modeling efficiency when working with very large projects, you can work with</ac:inline-comment-marker> partially loaded used projects. This may help decrease memory consumption.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="59c63b28-be91-452a-8029-9feff4e1b151"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Here, “large” is tens of thousands of classes and other complex elements. If counting all small elements, such as properties, methods, method parameters, several hundreds of thousands of elements is considered a large project. Note that diagrams are large elements. A project consisting of 1000 or more complex diagrams should be considered large.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>You can unload a particular used project at any time when working with a large partitioned project.</p><p>To unload a used project from a project</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the selected used project, and select <strong>Project Usages </strong>&gt; <strong>Unload</strong>.</li></ul><p><span>Not loaded (or unloaded) used projects can be loaded in a project anytime. For more information about managing used projects</span>, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Managing used projects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Managing Used Projects]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><span><br /></span></p><p><span>To load a used project in a project</span></p><hr /><ul><li>From the unloaded used project's shortcut menu, select <strong>Used Projects</strong> and then <strong>Load Used Project.</strong></li></ul><p>When the used project is neither loaded nor unloaded, there can be some model elements left from the used project. These elements are not editable and are characterized by a small M letter in the upper left corner of their icon (see the following figure). </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:class="panning" ac:alt="notation_of_unloaded_elements.png"><ri:url ri:value="https://jira.nomagic.com/secure/attachment/137835/notation_of_unloaded_elements.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);font-size: 12.0px;text-align: center;">Notation of unloaded used project elements (fragment of Containment tree)</span></h6><p><span>These are the so-called “proxy” elements of the original elements from the used project. Instead of the original model elements, the proxy is a lightweight surrogate that carries only the name and kind of the model element information. These proxies are left in place of the original used project elements and are dedicated to maintaining model integrity (ensuring that there are no dangling ends of relationships, types of properties do not disappear, etc.) while the used project is not accessible or not loaded. </span></p><p><span>There are 4 used project loading modes: </span></p><ul><li><strong>Always load </strong><span>(default). In this mode, used projects are always loaded when you open the project. They can be unloaded if necessary.</span></li><li><strong>Autoload</strong><span>. A used project is not loaded while the project using it is loaded. However, our modeling tool monitors user activities in the project and tries to guess when you might want to use the model piece from the unloaded used project. For example, if you search and find usages/dependencies, reports, metrics, transformations, or code engineering actions with a scope that touches the unloaded used project, our modeling tool will load that used project. </span></li><li><strong>Autoload with prompt</strong><span>. A used project is not loaded while the project using it is loaded. It can be loaded using the </span><span><strong>Load</strong> used project </span><span>command (described above).</span></li></ul><p><span>To change the used project loading mode</span></p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Used Projects.</strong> The <strong>Used Projects</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the used project's tree, select a used project.</li><li>In the <strong>Used Project Load Mode</strong> area, select the appropriate loading mode.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8984fff4-b130-43a8-b873-2cb153ba55e3"><ac:rich-text-body>Frequently Used Projects should be set in the <strong>Always load</strong> mode.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Occasionally used projects should be set in <strong>Autoload</strong> mode (or <strong>Autoload with prompt</strong> to have more control on loading behavior). Very rarely used projects can be set in the <strong>Manual load</strong> mode. Another frequent case where used projects can be set to <strong>Manual load</strong> mode occurs when the used projects represent a software library that is not expected to change.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1aebf624-bb84-401d-bcb5-c5d933a9aa0d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418017742</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maintaining decomposed model integrity" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967275 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06692: Working with paths and relationships

- page_id: `243967275`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967275/Working+with+paths+and+relationships
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T12:10:14.207+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['path', 'relationship', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The difference between relationships and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Paths' space='DEVG'] is that a relationship is a link between model elements, while the path is a representation of the relationship on a diagram.

You can learn how to work with paths and relationships on the following pages:

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying paths' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>The difference between relationships and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Paths" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[paths]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is that a relationship is a link between model elements, while the path is a representation of the relationship on a diagram.</p><p>You can learn how to work with paths and relationships on the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="5b8cf48e-517b-40c1-87ad-cbfb8cd4fd76" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="with-breadcrumbs" data-uuid="602ca897-207b-459a-bafc-5b522a687f79"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying paths" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564338 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06693: Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart

- page_id: `249564338`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564338/Working+with+Personnel+Availability+Gantt+Chart
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:51.201+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Personnel > Personnel Roadmap
- labels: ['personnel', 'availability', 'gantt', 'chart', 'fill-post', 'actual-post', 'actual-organization', 'actual-person', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

With the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart, you will be able to model your actual organization structure based on the typical organization structure and fill Actual Posts with one or more Actual Persons. You will be able to visualize how the actual posts are filled, see which actual persons become available on a certain period of time.

The Personnel Availability Gantt Chart shows Actual Organizations and Actual Posts as rows. Each Actual Post can be filled by an Actual Person. When the Actual Post is filled by an Actual Person, the Fills Post relationship is created. The start and end dates can be defined for the Fills Post relationship. The start and end dates define the time frame for Actual Person filling the Actual Post. The same Actual Person can work in several positions.

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space=''] chapter.

##### Creating Personnel Availability Gantt Chart

When you choose to create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** opens.

To create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart from the wizard

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Personnel Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_personnel_gantt_chart.png']
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Personnel Availability Gantt Chart** check box.

To create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** is disabled

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart .
  - Right-click the Personnel Roadmap package and select Create Diagram . Select Personnel Availability Gantt Chart .
2. Type the chart name. (Optional)
3. Click Finish .

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard property value to true .

##### Adding Actual Organizations and Actual Posts

To add a new Actual Organization

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Actual Organization . [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_actual_organization.png']
2. Select a type for the Actual Organization. An empty line for the newly created Actual Organization appears on the diagram pane.

To add a new Actual Post

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Actual Post . [ATTACHMENT filename='add_new_actual_post.png']
2. Select a type for the Actual Post. An empty line for the newly created Actual Post appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Actual Organization

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing > Actual Organization .
2. In the Select Actual Organization dialog, select an Actual Organization and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

To add an existing Actual Post

1. On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing > Actual Post .
2. In the Select Actual Post dialog, select an Actual Post and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Sub Actual Organizations and Sub Actual Posts

To add a new Sub Actual Organizations

1. Select the Actual Organizations for which you want to create a Sub Actual Organizations.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Actual Organizations .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Organizations, select Add New Sub Actual Organizations .

To add a new Sub Actual Post

1. Select the Actual Post for which you want to create a Sub Actual Post.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Actual Post .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Post, select Add New Sub Actual Post .

To add an existing Sub Actual Organizations

1. Select the Actual Organizations for which you want to add a Sub Actual Organizations.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Actual Organizations .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Organization, select Add Existing Sub Actual Organizations .
3. From the Select Actual Organizations dialog, select an Actual Organizations and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

To add an existing Sub Actual Post

1. Select the Actual Post for which you want to add a Sub Actual Post.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Actual Post .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Actual Post, select Add Existing Sub Actual Post .
3. From the Select Actual Post dialog, select an Actual Post and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Actual Person

To add an Actual Person

1. Select an Actual Post for which you want to assign an Actual Person.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Actual Person .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Actual Post and select Add Actual Person . [ATTACHMENT filename='add_actual_person.png']
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Person Assignment Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

If you want to see each Actual Person in the separate row, in the chart Options area, select **Display Actual Person in Different Lines** check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Editing Actual Person

To edit an Actual Person

1. Right-click an Actual Post for which you want to edit an Actual Person.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Actual Person and click the Actual Person you want to edit. [ATTACHMENT filename='change_actual_person.png']
3. Edit the Start or End dates.
4. Click OK .

##### Removing Actual Person

To remove an Actual Person

1. Right-click an Actual Post for which you want to remove an Actual Person.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Remove Actual Person and click the Actual Person you want to remove. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_actual_person.png']

##### Modifying dates for Actual Person

You can modify the time frame of the Actual Person filling the Actual Post. You can do so in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']**Specification** window of the particular Fills Post relationship or directly in the chart.

It is quick and easy to modify dates directly in the chart:

- To change the start date, simply drag the Actual Person bar to the left or right.
- To change the end date, slide the end of the Actual Person bar to the left or right.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

##### Displaying all suppressed or removed from the chart

When an Actual Organization or Actual Post is displayed in *italic*, it means that some sub elements were removed from the chart. You can restore them back with the click of one button.

To display all sub elements removed from the chart

1. Select the Actual Organization (or Actual Post) which sub elements you want to restore.
2. In the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

The **Display All** button also expands the suppressed structure.

##### Deleting elements and sub elements

You can either remove the element or sub element from the chart or delete it from the model.

To delete an element or sub elementfrom the chart

1. Select an element or sub element .
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete an element or sub elementfrom the model

1. Select an element or sub element .
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="77f963b1-f3a9-411d-904d-956014199002" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>With the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart, you will be able to model your actual organization structure based on the typical organization structure and fill Actual Posts with one or more Actual Persons. You will be able to visualize how the actual posts are filled, see which actual persons become available on a certain period of time.</p><p>The Personnel Availability Gantt Chart shows Actual Organizations and Actual Posts as rows. Each Actual Post can be filled by an Actual Person. When the Actual Post is filled by an Actual Person, the Fills Post relationship is created. The start and end dates can be defined for the Fills Post relationship. The start and end dates define the time frame for Actual Person filling the Actual Post. The same Actual Person can work in several positions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95a283d7-ac16-44af-bffb-bfda138b8c03"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link> chapter.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><br />Creating Personnel Availability Gantt Chart</h3><p>When you choose to create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart, <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong> and select <strong>Personnel Availability Gantt Chart</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the Personnel Roadmap package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>. Select <strong>Personnel Availability Gantt Chart</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_personnel_gantt_chart.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1ccb972-4d50-438b-9600-348f9c84a65a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" create Personnel Availability Gantt Chart</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Personnel Availability Gantt Chart manually when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main toolbar, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong> and ac:macro-id="8600af1e-c713-4272-82fa-719de9571ce4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding Actual Organizations and Actual Posts</h3><p>To add a new Actual Organization</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Organization</strong>.<br click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Actual Post</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="250" ac:width="538"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_new_actual_post.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select a type for the Actual Post.<br />An empty line for the newly created Actual Post appears on the diagram pane.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Actual Organization</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Personnel Availability Gantt Chart toolbar, click<strong> Add Existing </strong>&gt;<strong> Actual Organization</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Actual Organization </strong>dialog, select an Actual Organization and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564292 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 06694: Working with Personnel Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249564292`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564292/Working+with+Personnel+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:11.944+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Connectivity diagram.

To create a typical form of Personnel Connectivity diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, and Persons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an actual form of Personnel Connectivity diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competence of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Connectivity diagram.</p><p> </p><p>To create a typical form of Personnel Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an actual form of Personnel Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564299 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06695: Working with Personnel Connectivity Table

- page_id: `249564299`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564299/Working+with+Personnel+Connectivity+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:47.148+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Personnel Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add new or existing Resource Exchange to fill in an Personnel Connectivity Table.

##### Adding new Resource Exchanges

To add new Resource Exchange

1. In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Click + button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:
  - Resource Exchange
  - Command
  - Control
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.
- Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Personnel Connectivity Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchange to fill in an Personnel Connectivity Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Personnel Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><strong>+</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> button to select one of the following Resource Exchange types:</span><br /><ul><li>Resource Exchange</li><li>Command</li><li>Control</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table.</li><li>Sending Resource, Receiving Resource, and Implements cells are read only.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></span> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Constraints Definition diagram.

To create a typical form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Organizations, Posts, and Persons.
2. Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.

To create an actual form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.
2. Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.

You can display:

- the Competence of the Organizational Resources.
- the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Typical and Actual organizational relationships as well as relationships between Competence and Responsibility, are modeled in Personnel Constraints Definition diagram.</p><p> </p><p>To create a typical form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Organizations, <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Posts, and <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Persons</span></span>.</span></span></li><li>Display the generalizations (general-specific relationships) and compositions (whole-part relationships) between them.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To create an actual form of Personnel Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons.</li><li>Relate Actual Organizational Resources with Actual Organization Relationship.</li></ol><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="439ad953-f2b7-48c9-9049-4b629719adf6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display:</p><ul><li>the <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Competence </span></span>of the Organizational Resources.</li><li>the Operational Resource that is in charge of others using the Commands relationship.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564308 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06697: Working with Personnel Constraints table

- page_id: `249564308`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564308/Working+with+Personnel+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:12.656+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Personnel > Personnel Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding a new Resource

1. Click the Add New button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the cell and in the Select Subject Of Resource Constraint dialog, choose Resource Artifact, Software, Capability Configuration, Organization , Post, Function, Data Element, or Resource Exchange.
3. Define the **Rule Specification**. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.
4. Select the Rule Kind from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.

To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding and existing Resource

- Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding a new Resource</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add New </strong>button to add a new Resource Constraint. The new row in the table is added.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image></span> in the cell and in the <strong>Select Subject Of Resource Constraint</strong> dialog, choose <span>Resource Artifact,</span> Software, Capability Configuration, Organization<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Post, Function, Data Element, or Resource Exchange</span>. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Define the <strong>Rule Specification</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8d7218cc-0982-4a1e-a68c-e9dde5ebd074"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Select the <strong>Rule Kind</strong> from one of the following:<ul><li>Structural Assertion</li><li>Action Assertion</li><li>Derivation</li><li>Contract</li><li>Constraint</li><li>Guidance</li><li>Security Policy</li><li>Caveat</li></ul></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f10bb7bd-1c36-41bc-a773-c181b23d6555"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can assign more than one Resource for one constraint.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Personnel Constraints table by adding and existing Resource</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Resource Constraints in the model.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564331 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06698: Working with Personnel Evolution diagram

- page_id: `249564331`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564331/Working+with+Personnel+Evolution+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:50.341+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Personnel > Personnel Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Personnel Evolution diagram

To create a Personnel Evolution diagram

1. Create new Version Of Configuration:
  1. Click the Add New button and select Version of Configuration.
  2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.
2. Assign the resource for the Version Of Configuration:
  1. Right-click the Version Of Configuration in the diagram pane and select Resource .
  2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Assignment Wizard' space=''] .
  3. Click Finish when you are done. The resource is assigned for Version Of Configuration in specified time frame.

##### Removing Resources from Personnel Evolution diagram

To remove a Resource

1. Right-click the Resource bar and from the shortcut menu, select Remove Resource > <name of the resource you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from Personnel Evolution diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']

&lt;name of the resource you want to remove&gt;.</li></ol><h3>Removing Version of Configurations from Personnel Evolution diagram</h3><p>To delete a Version of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Version of Configuration.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564334 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06699: Working with Personnel Forecast diagram

- page_id: `249564334`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564334/Working+with+Personnel+Forecast+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:50.742+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Personnel > Personnel Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658918461

1658918466

1658918465

To create a Personnel Forecast table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658918459

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564294 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06700: Working with Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249564294`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564294/Working+with+Personnel+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:46.694+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Personnel > Personnel Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

The Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Operational Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be completed.

To create a Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchangein Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog that enables to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Personnel Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
