<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564642 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06739: Working with Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `249564642`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564642/Working+with+Risks+to+Assets+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:13.773+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282882341

1282882352

1282882342

The Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix****(Sc-Tr) shows which risks are applicable to Asset Roles. The Rowsof this matrix are Risks and the Columns are Assets.

To build the matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Risks);
2. Specify the Column Scope (Assets);
3. Click the Refresh button.

To map a Risks to an Assets

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Affects.

To remove the relation from the matrix

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282882340

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9431c234-01f2-4c3b-af8e-294368fa6a41"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282882341</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b10731a-1ae7-4020-8574-c38d01083f18"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282882352</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11001635-10b1-4045-9b18-1af6c421da58"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282882342</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Risks to Assets Mapping Matrix<strong> </strong>(Sc-Tr) shows which risks are applicable to Asset Roles. <span>The Rows</span><span> of this matrix are Risks and the Columns are Assets.</span></p><p> </p><p>To build the matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Risks);</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Assets);</li><li>Click the <strong>Refresh</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To map a Risks to an Assets</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.</li><li>Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Affects.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation from the matrix</p><hr /><ul><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c11f1236-c4e0-4169-9f70-47c912a29150"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1282882340</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with matrices" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563597 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06741: Working with S1 Service Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `249563597`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563597/Working+with+S1+Service+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:59.637+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Service Taxonomy Table (S1)**provides all the available service types.

To add a new Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button and choose Service or Service Architecture. A new row is added.
2. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Service or Service Architecture.

To add an existing Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table

1. In the diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Service dialog, choose the desired Services or Service Architectures.
3. Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Services or Service Architectures.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The<span> </span><strong>Service Taxonomy Table (S1)</strong><span> </span>provides all the available service types.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Add New</strong><span> </span>button and choose Service or Service Architecture. A new row is added.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Service or Service Architecture.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Service or Service Architecture in the Service Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Service</strong> dialog, choose the desired Services or Service Architectures.</li><li>Double-click the cells to enter a Name, Definition, and URI for the Services or Service Architectures.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563594 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06742: Working with S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249563594`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563594/Working+with+S1+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:15:59.557+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > S1 Service Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **S1****Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>S1 </strong><strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a S1 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" /></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564565 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06743: Working with Security Connectivity (Operational) Table

- page_id: `249564565`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564565/Working+with+Security+Connectivity+Operational+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:08.475+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A **Security Connectivity (Operational) Table** represents exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add new or existing Operational Exchanges to fill in a Security Connectivity (Operational) Table.

##### Adding new Operational Exchanges

To add new Operational Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges

To add an existing Operational Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Operational Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select needed Operational Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities

1. In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Activities button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Activities dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A <strong>Security Connectivity (Operational) Table</strong> represents exchanges between Operational Agents. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges to fill in a Security Connectivity (Operational) Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Operational Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Operational Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Security Connectivity (Operational) Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Operational Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select needed Operational Exchanges.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchange Identifier, Operational Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other<span> cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Operational Exchange data.</span></span><span> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target" style="">Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="">To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li>In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the<span> </span><strong>Activities</strong><span> </span>button.</li><li>In the open<span> </span><strong>Producing and Consuming Activities<span> </span></strong>dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564559 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06744: Working with Security Connectivity (Resource) Table

- page_id: `249564559`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564559/Working+with+Security+Connectivity+Resource+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:07.018+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Security Connectivity (Resource) Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add new or existing Resource Exchange to fill in a Security Connectivity (Resource) Table.

##### Adding new Resource Exchanges

To add new Resource Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity (Resource) Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges

To add an existing Resource Exchange

1. In the Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Resource Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select needed Resource Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions

1. In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Functions dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Security Connectivity (Resource) Table represents exchanges between Resources. You can add <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchange to fill in a Security Connectivity (Resource) Table.</p><h3>Adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add new Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Security Connectivity (Resource) Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add New </strong>button. The <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Resource Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Resource Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with created Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the  Security Connectivity Table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Resource Exchange</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select needed Resource Exchanges.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.<br /><br /></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchange Identifier, Resource Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other<span> cells are read-only.</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can use</span> the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> that enables you to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.</span></span><span> </span><br /></span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.</li><li>In the open<span> </span><strong>Producing and Consuming Functions</strong><span> </span>dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564543 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06745: Working with Security Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249564543`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564543/Working+with+Security+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:05.386+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating Resources Connectivity diagram

The Security Connectivity diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be completed.

To create a Security Connectivity diagram

1. Create resources.
2. Connect resources using Association.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Associations.

The Security Connectivity product also allows you to show the Capabilities. You can model internal structure of each resource directly in the compartment area of this element shape or using the Security Structure diagram.

You can proceed to create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Security Processes' space=''] as soon as the Resources Connectivity has been completed.

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Security Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in Security Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Association between a pair of resources and on the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] .
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and on the diagram pane, connect resources.
  - Click the Control relationship and connect Organizational Resources.
  - Click the Command relationship and connect Physical Resources.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Data Element can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager**dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchange data.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564570 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06746: Working with Security Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `249564570`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564570/Working+with+Security+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:08.994+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1163362754

1163362771

1163362753

**On this page**

1163362755

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Security Constraints Definition diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Constraints package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Constraints Definition . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_security_constraints_definition.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Constraints Definition and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements (assets). An example is described using the Security Enclave element, but the same is valid for other assets.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_security_enclave.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Security Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating Resource Exchanges in Security Constraints Definition diagram

To create a Resource Exchange

1. Open the **Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the asset (e.g. Security Enclave) and from its smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange.png'] and then click another asset to connect them.
  - Click [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_exchange_palette.png'] and on the diagram palette, and connect the assets.
2. Specify the Exchange Name, Resource Exchange Type.
3. Select the Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Geo Political Extent Exchange
  - Resource Communication
  - Resource Energy Flow
  - Resource Movement
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Creating and applying Security Constraints

You can create the Security Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See . Also, you can create constraints in the selected asset's Specification window.

To create a Security Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed asset and open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Security > Security Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

There are the following constrained elements that can be a subject of the Security Constraint:

1. Asset
2. Asset Role
3. Exchange
4. Security Process

To apply Security Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Security Constraint and drag it on the element.
- In the needed element's Specification window, go to Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564579 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06747: Working with Security Constraints table

- page_id: `249564579`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564579/Working+with+Security+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:20.109+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Security Constraints** table identifies the constraints that are imposed on systems functionality due to some aspects of system design or implementation.

To create a Security Constraints table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Select Subject Of Security Constraint** dialog, choose the needed constrained element(s):
  - Asset
  - Asset Role
  - Exchange
  - Security Process
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Security Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Security Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Security Constraints table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>and  in the <strong>Select Subject Of Security Constraint</strong> dialog, choose the needed constrained element(s):</span><br /><ul><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Asset Role</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Exchange</span></li><li><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper">Security Process</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<strong> Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify the body.<br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd8254de-6c02-4462-a6df-36afcf72fabc"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p><p>To add an existing Security Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select Security Constraints. ````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564646 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06748: Working with Security Controls diagram

- page_id: `249564646`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564646/Working+with+Security+Controls+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:42.661+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Motivation
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Security Controls diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Motivation and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Controls . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_security_controls.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Controls and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. To illustrate the example, Security Control and Risk elements are used, but you can also choose Enhances Security Control, Security Control Family, or Enhances as well as Security Risk.

To create a Security Control in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click Security Control and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create a Security Control in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Security Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Select Security Control .
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability.

##### Connect the elements

When you have Security Control created, you can start connecting them using the Mitigates relationship.

To connect the elements with the Mitigates relationship

1. Select an element from which the Mitigates will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Mitigates. You can also select the Mitigates relationship from the diagram palette.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564640 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06749: Working with Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix

- page_id: `249564640`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564640/Working+with+Security+Controls+to+Risks+Mapping+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:13.347+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282871885

1282871887

1282871886

The Security Controls to Risks Mapping Matrix****(Sc-Tr) shows which operational or resource asset roles mitigate risks. The Rows of this matrix are Security Controls and the Columns are Risks.

To build the matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Security Controls);
2. Specify the Column Scope (Risks);
3. Click the Refresh button.

To map an Security Controls to a Risk

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Mitigate.

To remove the relation from the matrix

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282871884

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564547 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06750: Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

- page_id: `249564547`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564547/Working+with+Security+Internal+Connectivity+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:05.933+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating a Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

The Resource Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be completed.

To create a Resource Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

1. Create Operational Roles and Operational Ports.
2. Associate Operational Roles using Operational Connectors.
3. Create Operational Exchanges flowing via Operational Connectors.

##### Creating Operational Exchange in Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

To create an Operational Exchange in the Security Internal Connectivity (Operational) diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Operational Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_create_op_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_operational_exchange.png'] and then click the Operational Connector.
2. Select the Operational Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Configuration
  - Energy
  - Geo Political Extent
  - Information
  - Materiel
  - Organizational
3. Specify the Operational Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Operational Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Operational Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Operational Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Operational Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram with Operational Activities, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564554 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06751: Working with Security Internal Connectivity (Resource) diagram

- page_id: `249564554`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564554/Working+with+Security+Internal+Connectivity+Resource+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:06.495+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating a Security Internal Connectivity diagram

The Resource Structure diagram requires that the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Taxonomy diagram' space=''] should be completed.

To create a Resource Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Resource Roles and Resource Ports.
2. Associate Resource Roles using Resource Connectors.
3. Create Resource Exchanges flowing via Resource Connectors.

##### Creating Resource Exchange in Security Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Resource Exchange in the Security Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Resource Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='security_resource_exchange.png'] and then click the Resource Connector.
2. Select the Resource Exchange Kind from one of the following:
  - Command (between Organizational Resources)
  - Control (between Physical Resources)
  - Resource Exchange
3. Specify the Resource Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction. Only Resource Information can be a Conveyed Item for the Command and Control relationships.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Functions.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Resource Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Resource Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Resource Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Resource Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram with Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Security Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564584 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06752: Working with Security Parametric. Operational diagram

- page_id: `249564584`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564584/Working+with+Security+Parametric.+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:10.034+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Parameters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496649791

496649793

496649792

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Mitigation. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Role or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Mitigation context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496649790

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fb01621-ad1b-4475-a253-2722d62b6638"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496649791</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e48c44e-752f-4ca9-a9af-3d9d4d33b568"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496649793</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf360b26-7caf-4b83-80c6-920889bbd6f2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496649792</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> to constrain the properties of another Operational Mitigation. It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Operational Role or Measurements) from within that internal Operational Mitigation context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b53a58cb-7204-4f58-9b52-b2c98c9c4f2f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496649790</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Resource Mitigation. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564596 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06754: Working with Security Process Flow (Operational) diagram

- page_id: `249564596`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564596/Working+with+Security+Process+Flow+Operational+diagram
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-08-22T09:09:38.167+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Security Process Flow diagram represents flows of security processes. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Processes diagram' space=''] must be created first.

The owner of the Security Process Flow diagram is always a Security Process.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram for Security Process Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Agent or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click Cancel in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Operational Agent or Operational Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Security Process Actions/Operational Activity Actions or drop Security Process/Operational Activities from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Security Process Actions/Operational Activity Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the operational structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Security Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created. You can add either Security Process Action or Operational Action in the Swimlane. You can create an inner Security Process Flow diagram for a Security Process Action or Operational Action Smart Manipulator toolbar in both cases.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram from a Smart Manipulator of a Security Process Action or Operational Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Security Process Action or Operational Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Security Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Operational Agent or Operational Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have an operational structure defined.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Security Process Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Operational Agent and the Security Process (the behavior of the Security Process Action). Furthermore, if the Operational Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Security Process Action.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']

## PAGE 06755: Working with Security Process Flow (Resources) diagram

- page_id: `249564592`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564592/Working+with+Security+Process+Flow+Resources+diagram

- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Security Process Flow diagram represents flows of security processes. Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Security Processes diagram' space=''] must be created first.

The owner of Security Process Flow diagram is always a Security Process.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram for Security Process Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel**in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Resource Performer or Resource Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Security Process Actions/Function Actions or drop Security Process/Function from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Security Process Actions/Function Actions wit h Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.
4. Display the possible Operational Exchanges on Operational Control Flow or Operational Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Security Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Security Process Action or Function Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Security Process Flow diagram for a Security Process Action or Function Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a Security Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Security Process Action or Function Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Security Process Action or Function Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Security Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Resource Performer or Resource Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new SecurityProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Security Process Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Resource Performer and Security Process (the behavior of the Security Process Action). Furthermore, if the Resource Role is assigned to the Swimlane, the PerformInContext relationship is created between the role and Security Process Action.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564590 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06756: Working with Security Processes diagram

- page_id: `249564590`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564590/Working+with+Security+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:20.446+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies

1. Create Security Process
2. Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Security Process</li><li>Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564563 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06757: Working with Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table

- page_id: `249564563`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564563/Working+with+Security+Role-based+Connectivity+Operational+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:07.991+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table** specifies the characteristics of the data exchange between Operational Roles. You can fill in a table by adding new or existing Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors.

If you choose to add an Operational Connector, all Operational Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.

##### Adding existing Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table

To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connector

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select the needed Operational Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.

- Operational Exchanges Identifier, Operational Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Operational Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Operational Exchangedata.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities

1. In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Activities button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Activities dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can fill in a table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa3b050a-eccc-49a2-bd41-45a3c042f58d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you choose to add an Operational Connector, all Operational Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Adding existing Operational Exchanges or Operational Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity (Operational) Table</h3><p>To add an existing Operational Exchange or Operational Connector</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the dialog, </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">select the needed Operational Exchanges.</span></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Operational Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Operational Exchanges Identifier, Operational Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Operational <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Modifying Producing and Consuming Activities</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Activities for the selected Operational Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To modify the Producing and Consuming Activities</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the needed Operational Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Activities</strong> button.</li><li>In the open <strong>Producing and Consuming Activities </strong>dialog, review the Activities and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564561 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06758: Working with Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table

- page_id: `249564561`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564561/Working+with+Security+Role-based+Connectivity+Resource+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:07.506+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table** specifies the characteristics of the data exchange between Resource Roles. You can fill in a table by adding new or existing Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors.

If you choose to add a Resource Connector, all Resoutce Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.

##### Adding existing Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity Table

To add an existing Resource Exchange or Resource Connector

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog select needed Resource Exchanges.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.

- Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only.
- You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Resource Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Resource Exchangedata.

##### Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions

You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).

To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions

1. In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.
3. In the open Producing and Consuming Functions dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Security Role-based Connectivity (Resource) Table</strong> specifies the characteristics of the data exchange between Resource Roles. You can fill in a table by adding <span class="confluence-link">new</span> or <span class="confluence-link">existing</span> Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa3b050a-eccc-49a2-bd41-45a3c042f58d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you choose to add a Resource Connector, all Resoutce Exchanges flowing via that connector will be added to the table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Adding existing Resource Exchanges or Resource Connectors in a Security Role-based Connectivity Table</h3><p>To add an existing Resource Exchange or Resource Connector</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Element</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the dialog select needed Resource Exchanges.</span></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br />The rows with selected Resource Exchanges are added to the table.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dd42d46f-003d-42ce-ae5f-8b5fda76b70e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Resource Exchanges Identifier, Resource Exchanges Item Name, Producing and Consuming Functions, and a wide range of measurement cells are allowed to edit in the table. All other cells are read-only. </li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Resource Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Resource <span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span></span>data.</span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Modifying Producing and Consuming Functions</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">You can modify the Producing and Consuming Functions for the selected Resource Exchange (if they are created).</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To modify the Producing and Consuming Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table, click the needed Resource Exchange.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the Functions button.</li><li>In the open <strong>Producing and Consuming Functions</strong> dialog, review the Functions and remove the ones you don't need.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564605 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06759: Working with Security Structure diagram

- page_id: `249564605`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564605/Working+with+Security+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:12.618+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Security Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Security Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Security Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Resource Mitigation element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Resource Mitigation in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Resource Mitigation and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Resource Mitigation in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Security Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Resource Mitigation and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_resource_mitigation.png']

Resource Mitigation

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Resource Mitigation (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Resource Mitigation

When you have Resource Mitigation created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and when it is highlighted in blue, click to finish the action.

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Resource Roles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Directed Composition** and choose **Directed** **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.display_related.png']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='security_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. 
  1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
  2. Do one of the following:
    - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
    - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
  3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
  4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='security.str.display_related_nested.png']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="724" ac:width="715"><ri:attachment ri:filename="security.str.display_related_nested.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564624 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06760: Working with Security Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249564624`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564624/Working+with+Security+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:42.158+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1159118804

1159118806

1159118803

**On this page**

1159118805

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Security Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Security Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Security Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_security_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Security Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Security Enclave element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_security_enclave.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Security Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Security Enclave (or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564634 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06761: Working with Security Taxonomy table

- page_id: `249564634`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564634/Working+with+Security+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:21.301+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Security > Security Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Security Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button and select Capability Configuration, Data Element, Information Element, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer, Organization, Person, Post, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, or Technology. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Asset dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<div class="wiki-content"><p>To create a Security Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button and select Capability Configuration, Data Element, Information Element, Known Resource, Natural Resource, Operational Architecture, Operational Mitigation, Operational Performer, Organization, Person, Post, Resource Architecture, Resource Artifact, Resource Mitigation, Responsibility, Security Enclave, Software, System, or Technology. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Asset</strong> dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564229 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06762: Working with Service Sequences diagram

- page_id: `249564229`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564229/Working+with+Service+Sequences+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T14:01:47.930+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Sequences
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Service Exchange in the Service Sequences diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized****Service Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Service Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Service Roles, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click[IMAGE alt='' src=''] .
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and on the diagram palette select the Service Message.
2. Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchanges data.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Service Exchange in the Service Sequences diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"> </span><span class="confluence-link"><strong>Realized </strong><strong>Service Exchange</strong> wizard</span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span class="confluence-link"> </span> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">On the diagram pane, select the Service Message between a pair of two Lifelines of Service Roles, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange.png" /></ac:image></span>.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange_palette.png" /></ac:image>and on the diagram palette select the Service Message.</span></li></ul></li><li>Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.</li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p>You can use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchanges data.</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
