<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564161 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06763: Working with Services Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249564161`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564161/Working+with+Services+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:39.005+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Services Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Services Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_connectivity_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Services Connectivity and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Service element, but the same is valid for other Service specialization elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Service Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_service_to_diagram.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or other appropriate elements).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

A Services Connectivity view also allows you to show mappings among Capabilities and Services, Physical Location requirements, Operational Performers.

The structure of each Service can be modeled using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram' space=''].

##### Creating Service Exchanges

Before creating Service Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Operational Information, Service Signal, Resource Performer). Then you can start creating Service Exchanges.

To create a Service Exchange in the Service Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select a Service and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Service Exchange button. Click another Service to connect. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_exchange_smart_manipulator.png']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Service Exchange**and on the diagram pane, connect Services. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
4. Click Finish when you are done.

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog that enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchanges data.

##### Realizing Service Exchanges on Associations

When you have Services connected with an Association, there are two ways to create service exchanges:

- Opening Exchange Manager.
- Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.

###### Opening the Exchange Manager

To create a new Service Exchange on the Association

1. Click the Association between Services.
2. In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Service Exchange. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_exchange_on_association.png']
3. Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.
4. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. ( Optional )
5. Click Finish when you are done. [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange_realized_on_association.png']

Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog.

To open a **Service Exchange Manager** dialog

1. Selectan Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.
2. In the smart manipulator, click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_resource_exchange.png'] . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange_manager_from_smart_manipulator.png']

Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Service Exchanges on the Association.

###### Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association

You can create the Service Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.

In the wizard, specify the needed information.

In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Search for the Service and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="404" ac:width="350"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="289" ac:width="848"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_service_to_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d7d01530-058c-4324-a12b-1096609019f2"><ac:rich-text-body>The structure of each Service can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Service Internal Connectivity diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>.</span></p><h3>Creating Service Exchanges</h3><p>Before creating Service Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Operational Information, Service Signal, Resource Performer). Then you can start creating Service Exchanges.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Service Exchange in the Service Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select a Service and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Service Exchange button. Click another Service to connect.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_exchange_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></li><li> In the diagram palette, c<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">lick <strong>Service Exchange </strong>and on the diagram pane, connect Services.</span> <br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="132"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_exchange_pallete.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_exchange_wizard.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="16de18c6-2f39-40a4-a2f2-b9b40b154023"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. (<em>Optional</em>)</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4c4befc-85e4-439e-84bb-0c6c8f57299d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> that enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchanges data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Realizing Service Exchanges on Associations</h3><p>When you have Services connected with an Association, there are two ways to create service exchanges:</p><ul><li>Opening Exchange Manager.</li><li>Dragging the Exchange Items on an Association.</li></ul><h4>Opening the Exchange Manager</h4><p>To create a new Service Exchange on the Association</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the Association between Services.</li><li>In the smart manipulator toolbar, click New Service Exchange. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_exchange_on_association.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Kind, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_exchange_wizard.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1810a3e-531e-49fa-b3c9-57d324bb8ed3"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Conveyed Item depends on the selected Exchange Kind.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Activities. (<em>Optional</em>)</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange_realized_on_association.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>Once an Association Relationship is created between two structural elements, all existing exchanges between the elements are visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open a <strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Select </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">an Association (also Operational Connector, Operational Control Flow, Operational Object Flow, or Operational Message) in the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li>In the smart manipulator, click <ac:image ac:title="New Resource Exchange" ac:alt="New Resource Exchange"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new_resource_exchange.png" /></ac:image>.<br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange_manager_from_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>Click to select or clear the check box in the Realize column to show (realize) or hide Service Exchanges on the Association.</p><h4>Dragging the Exchange Item on the Association</h4><p>You can create the Service Exchanges on the Association by dragging the Exchange Elements onto the Association. When you drop the Exchange Element on the Association, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</p><p>In the wizard, specify the needed information. </p><p>In the Operational Exchange drop-down list, the existing Operational Exchanges will be listed.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564190 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06764: Working with Services Connectivity matrix

- page_id: `249564190`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564190/Working+with+Services+Connectivity+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:40.669+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Services Connectivity Matrix (Sv-Cn) show how services interact with each other. It is an editable matrix where cells represent Service Exchanges and the headers represent Services.

To create a Services Connectivity Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope (Services);
2. Specify Column Scope (Services);
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

To add new Service Exchange

1. Double-click (or right-click) an empty intersection. The Add New Service Exchange button appears.
2. Click the Add New Service Exchange button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish .

To remove the exchange

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Services Connectivity Matrix (Sv-Cn) show how services interact with each other. It is an editable matrix where cells represent Service Exchanges and  the headers represent Services.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Services Connectivity Matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify<strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services);</li><li>Specify <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Services);</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" /></ac:image></span>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add new Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click (or right-click) an empty intersection. The <strong>Add New Service Exchange</strong> button appears.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add New Service Exchange</strong> button. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564184 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06765: Working with Services Connectivity table

- page_id: `249564184`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564184/Working+with+Services+Connectivity+table
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:39.872+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

**The Services Connectivity Table (Sv-Cn)** addresses service exchanges between services.

Service Exchanges in this table

##### Adding new Service Exchanges

To add new Service Exchange

1. In the Services Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add New button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Services Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.
3. Click Finish when you are done. The rows with created Service Exchanges are added to the table.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Service Connectivity Table toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Service Exchange dialog opens.
2. Select Service Exchanges by clicking the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. The rows with selected Service Exchanges are added to the table.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurement cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) diagram.
- You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialogthat enables you to analyze and manage the Service Exchange data.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the Service Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Service Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Service Connectivity Table report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

You can generate the report:</span></p><ul><li><span>Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the <strong>Report </strong>button,</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Using a quick report option from the Containment tree</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_connectivity_table_report.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of the Service Connectivity Table report</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

## PAGE 06766: Working with Services Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `249564199`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564199/Working+with+Services+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:09.453+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Services Constraints Definition diagram

1. Create Service.
2. Connect Service using generalizations (general-specific relationships).

The Services Constraints Definition diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service mapping.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Services Constraints Definition diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Service.</li><li>Connect Service using generalizations (general-specific relationships).</li></ol><p>The Services Constraints Definition diagram also allows you to show the Capabilities and Service mapping.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564196 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06767: Working with Services Constraints table

- page_id: `249564196`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564196/Working+with+Services+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:09.352+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Service Constraints** (Sv-Ct) table specifies constraints applying to the implementation of Services, as well as constraints governing the use of one or more Services.

To create a Services Constraints table by adding a new Service Policy or Service Contract

1. Click the Add New button and choose either Service Policy or Service Contract. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and
  1. for the Service Policy, in the Select Service dialog, select constrained Services .
  2. for the Service Contract, in the **Select Operational Connector** dialog, select Operational Connector.
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification**cell,click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).
5. Select the **Rule Kind** from one of the following:
  - Structural Assertion
  - Action Assertion
  - Derivation
  - Contract
  - Constraint
  - Guidance
  - Security Policy
  - Caveat

Additional constrained elements can be added and edited for every row in the table.

To add an existing Service Policy or Service Contract

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In Select Elements dialog, select Service Policies or Service Contract. The filled row is added in table.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" Connector.</span></li></ol></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564175 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06768: Working with Services Internal Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249564175`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564175/Working+with+Services+Internal+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:39.437+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Services Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Services Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Create Service Roles .
2. Associate Service Roles using Service Connectors.
3. Create Service Exchanges flowing via Service Connectors.

##### Creating Service Exchange in Service Internal Connectivity diagram

To create a Service Exchangein the Service Internal Connectivity diagram

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard by doing one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select the Service Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='smart_manipulator_sevice_exchange.png'] .
  - On the diagram palette, click [ATTACHMENT filename='service_exchange.png'] and then click the Service Connector.
2. Specify the Service Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.
3. Click Next to specify Producing and Consuming Service Functions.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Service Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Service Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Service Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Service Exchange** wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can use [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Exchange Manager dialog' space='']**Service Exchange Manager** dialog to analyze and manage the Service Exchangedata.

#### PANEL: Learn more

Learn more

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Behavior to structure synchronization' space=''].** You cancheck the consistency of the Services Internal Connectivity diagram with Service Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Services Internal Connectivity diagram.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e5a9a26-ea99-485f-9432-561ba682cf53" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Creating Services Internal Connectivity diagram</h3><p>To create a Services Internal Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Roles</span>.</li><li>Associate <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Service Roles</span> using Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Connectors.</span><span> </span></li><li><span>Create Service Exchanges flowing via Service Connectors.</span></li></ol><h3><span>Creating Service Exchange in Service Internal Connectivity diagram</span></h3><p>To create a Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>in the Service Internal Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> by doing one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select the Service Connector, and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="smart_manipulator_sevice_exchange.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>On the diagram palette, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_exchange.png" /></ac:image>and then click the Service Connector.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /></span></p></li><li>Click <strong>Next</strong> to specify Producing and Consuming Service Functions.</li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></p><p>If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Service Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.</li><li>Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Service Exchange on the diagram pane. The<span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Service Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Service Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link></span><span> </span>opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>Finish<span> </span></strong>when you are done. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_conveyed_item_palette_services.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9633d181-1d83-44bc-a56f-363440b93552"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can use <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Service Exchange Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> to analyze and manage the Service <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchange </span>data.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bffa1fff-edd7-4def-ac4a-9e35f09b42a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Learn more</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ac:link>.</strong> You can</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> check the consistency of the Services Internal Connectivity diagram with Service Functions, automatically detect inconsistencies, and handle each inconsistency individually using solver actions to ensure the coherence of the Services Internal Connectivity diagram.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564203 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06769: Working with Services Parametric diagram

- page_id: `249564203`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564203/Working+with+Services+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:41.993+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Parameters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496918799

496918802

496918800

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of other Services. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Service Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Service context. All properties displayed other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.

496918797

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and Constraint Parameters as well as other properties (Service Roles or Measurements) from within that internal Service context. All properties displayed other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a Constraint Parameter or contain a property that is bound to a Constraint Parameter (through any number of containment levels). A Constraint Block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many Constraint Parameters.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfb492fb-c4ee-4dfd-a9cd-6e576fc5b528"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">496918797</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

To create a Services Processes diagram

1. Create Service Functions.
2. Draw compositions or aggregations (whole-part relationships) between the Service Functions.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564212 space=MT version=4 -->
## PAGE 06771: Working with Services Processes Flow diagram

- page_id: `249564212`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564212/Working+with+Services+Processes+Flow+diagram
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-08-22T09:11:21.009+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before creating this diagram, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Processes diagram' space=''] must be created first.

To create a Service Process Flow diagram for Service Function Action flows

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram palette, click the Swimlanes and then click the diagram pane. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Service or Service Role to be represented by the swimlane. If you click **Cancel** in the dialog, the empty Swimlanes will be created.
  - Drag the Service or Service Role from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane to create the Swimlane.
2. Create Service Function Action or drop Service Function from the Containment tree directly to the diagram pane.
3. Connect Service Function Action wit h Service Control Flow or Service Object Flow.

##### Creating the inner Process Flow diagrams

Before creating the inner Process Flow diagram, the Process diagram should be created, and the resource structure should be defined there.

Let's say you have a defined structure andcreate the Service Process Flow diagram with the top elementas a Swimlane representative.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ServiceProcess Flow diagram is created. In the Swimlane, you can add either Service Function Action or Service Action. In both cases, you can create an inner Service Process Flow diagram for a Service Function Action or Service Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To create a ServiceProcess Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Service Function Action or Service Action

1. In the Swimlane, select a needed Service Function Action or Service Action .
2. In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click Service Process Flow.
3. The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Service or Service Role to be represented by the swimlane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] The **Select Swimlane Representatives** dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.If you click **Cancel**, the empty diagram will be created.
4. Click **OK**. 
A new ServiceProcess Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.

When the Service Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Service and Service Function (the behavior of the Service Function Action).

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Swimlanes' space='MED']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

In both cases, you can create an inner <span>Service</span> Process Flow diagram for a Service Function Action or Service Action Smart Manipulator toolbar.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">To create a Service<span> </span>Process Flow diagram from Smart Manipulator of a Service Function Action or Service Action</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Swimlane, select a needed <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Service Function Action or Service Action</span>.</li><li>In the Smart Manipulator toolbar, click <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Service</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Process Flow.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose the Service or Service Role to be represented by the swimlane.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_process_flow_representatives.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8f13748-4ee0-4144-baa4-a2c8977ae01c"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <strong>Select Swimlane Representatives</strong> dialog is displayed only when you have a resource structure defined in the Process diagram.</li><li>If you click <strong>Cancel</strong>, the empty diagram will be created.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />A new <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Service</span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span>Process Flow diagram is created with a Swimlane and the selected performers as the representatives.</p></li></ol><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">When the Service Function Action is created in the Swimlane, the IsCapableToPerform relationship is created between the Service and Service Function (the behavior of the Service Function Action).</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Swimlanes" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564218 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06772: Working with Services Roadmap diagram

- page_id: `249564218`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564218/Working+with+Services+Roadmap+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:43.264+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To work with a Services Roadmap diagram

truenone

##### Create a new Version Of Configuration

To create a new Version Of Configuration in the Service Roadmap diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the Service and its specializationsfor the Version Of Configuration

To define the Service and its specializationsfor the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram pane, select a Version of Configuration, and in the diagram toolbar, click **Add New** > **Service**.
  - In the diagram pane, right-click a Version Of Configuration and select **Service**.
  - In the Containment tree, select a Service and its specializationsand drag it onto the Version of Configuration. (In this case, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens in the second step.) The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Service and its specializationsfrom a Services Roadmap diagram

To remove a Service and its specializations

1. Right-click the Service and its specializations bar, and from the shortcut menu, select Remove > Service <name of the Service and its specializations you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564187 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06773: Working with Services Role-based Connectivity table

- page_id: `249564187`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564187/Working+with+Services+Role-based+Connectivity+table
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:40.280+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The Services Role-based Connectivity Table (Sv-Cn)** specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between services.

##### Adding existing Service Exchanges

To add an existing Service Exchange

1. In the Services Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button. The Select Elements dialog opens.
2. Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table. For multiple selection, click the **Multiple Selection** button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click **+** button.In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.
- Other cells are read-only.
- To create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.

##### Adding Service Functions

To add Service Functions

1. In the Services Role-based Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.
2. In the table toolbar, click the Service Functions button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] dialog opens.
3. In the open dialog, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Producing and Consuming Functions dialog' space=''] .
4. Click OK .

##### Generating Reports

The Service Role-based Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the **Report**button,
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Service Role-based Connectivity Table report

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>The Services Role-based Connectivity Table (Sv-Cn)</strong> specifies the characteristics of the data exchanged between services.</p><h3>Adding existing Service Exchanges</h3><p>To add an existing Service Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Services Role-based Connectivity diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. The<strong> Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Double-click Service Exchange or Service Connector from the list to add them to the table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48f021b3-2cf1-43b8-8ca7-31a54cac7bdd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For multiple selection, click the <strong>Multiple Selection</strong> button in the right bottom of the dialog, select Service Exchange / Service Connector and click <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>In case a Service Connector is selected, all the Service Exchanges flowing via it will be added to the table.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d729d05-0c85-4be4-9eeb-1480ee7dd983"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Service Exchange Identifier, Service Exchange Item Name, Producing and Consuming Activities, and a wide range of measurements cells are editable in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Other cells are read-only.</span></li><li>To <span>create and modify the Service Exchanges, you can use</span> Services Structure (Sv-Sr) diagram.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding Service Functions</h3><p>To add Service Functions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Services Role-based Connectivity Table, click a Service Exchange row for which you want to add Service Functions.</li><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Service Functions</strong> button. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Producing and Consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Producing and Consuming Functions dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[choose the producing and consuming Service Functions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Generating Reports</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The Service Role-based Connectivity Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:</span></p><ul><li><span>Directly from the table toolbar, clicking the <strong>Report </strong>button,</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Using a quick report option from the Containment tree</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="service_role_based_connectivity_table_report.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of the Service Role-based Connectivity Table report</span></h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564240 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06774: Working with Services Structure diagram

- page_id: `249564240`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564240/Working+with+Services+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:44.604+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### topCreating a diagram

To create a Services Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Services Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Services Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Service element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create a Service in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create a Service in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the Services Structure package, and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Service and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_service_specification.png']

Service

To create the elements from other resources (e.g., Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service (or another appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Services

When Service is created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the ServiceRoles (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity are shown.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Aggregation End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Directed Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.set_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the Specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. Using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you use these notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.display_related.png']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Select the element and click the Compartments, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='services_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such a case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties onto the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='services.str.display_related_nested.png']

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with exchanges' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564258 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06775: Working with Services Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249564258`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564258/Working+with+Services+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:34.768+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1160452963

1160452966

1160452961

**On this page**

1160452965

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Services Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Services Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Services Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_services_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Services Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Service Specification element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_service_specification.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Services Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Service Specification (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Service Specification (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c7bf3535-e44f-451e-b2d1-0cc8a1c2270b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452963</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="464f2ced-5f48-4f87-ac0a-5a6c3aebaec5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452966</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2c7201c0-f59d-40bc-84e1-3f12670ab88b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452961</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0e8e0af5-f0bb-4b00-b75e-1b22bb933699" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adfd81ac-5288-4cb6-8c91-56c59d94216d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1160452965</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create an Services Taxonomy diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Services Taxonomy and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Services Taxonomy</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="281" ac:width="313"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_services_taxonomy.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Services Taxonomy </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Service Specification element, but the same is valid for other elements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the Service Specification (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="259" ac:width="506"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_specification.png" /></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an element in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right click the Services Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Search for the Service Specification </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(or other appropriate element)</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="486" ac:width="310"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_service_specification_containment.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="220" ac:width="751"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_service_specification.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Service Specification (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have Service Specification (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc7b57ae-a1f8-48e0-811e-28d2602359f3"><ac:rich-text-body>You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_pallet_services.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.</span></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="257" ac:width="988"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generalization_smart_services.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p><p>If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.</p><p><br /></p><p>To make an element abstract</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Make sure that <strong>All</strong> is chosen for <strong>Properties</strong>.</li><li>Search for <strong>Is Abstract</strong> and set it to <em>true</em>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_is_abstract.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To view the inherited properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element and open its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>.</li><li>Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="services_inherited.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564268 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06776: Working with Services Taxonomy table

- page_id: `249564268`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564268/Working+with+Services+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:11.175+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Services Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button, and in the Select Service dialog, select the desired Service. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Services Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button, and in the <strong>Select Service </strong>dialog, select the desired Service. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564275 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06777: Working with Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249564275`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564275/Working+with+Services+to+Capabilities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:45.257+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282964251

1282964262

1282964261

The Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix (Sv-Tr) depicts mapping between the Capabilities and the Services that these Capabilities enable.

The Rows of this matrix are Services and the Columns are Capabilities.

To create a Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify Row Scope ( Service ).
2. Specify Column Scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Serviceexposes Capabilities using the Exhibitsrelationship.

To map aServiceto a Capability

- Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

- Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282964250

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564278 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06778: Working with Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249564278`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564278/Working+with+Services+to+Operational+Activities+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:45.678+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1282974676

1282974687

1282974677

The Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix (Sv-Tr) purpose is to provide traceability by illustrating which Services support which Operational Activities.

The Rows of this matrix are Servicesand the Columns are Operational Activities.

To create a Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Operational Activities);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Operational Activities map to Services using a Supports relationship.

To map Operational Activitiesto Services

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

1282974666

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564281 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06779: Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249564281`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564281/Working+with+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:11.554+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services > Services Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" /></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563668 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06780: Working with Sr Services Roadmap

- page_id: `249563668`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563668/Working+with+Sr+Services+Roadmap
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:17.636+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint > Sr Service Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921716523

INLINE

921716525

INLINE

921716524

INLINE

To work with a Services Roadmap diagram

truenone

##### Create a new Version Of Configuration

To create a new Version Of Configuration in the Service Roadmap diagram

1. Click the**Add New**button and selectVersion of Configuration.
2. Type the name of the newly created Version of Configuration.

##### Define the Service for the Version Of Configuration

To define the resource for the Version Of Configuration

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the diagram pane, select a Version of Configuration, and in the diagram toolbar, click **Add New** > **Service**.
  - In the diagram pane, right-click a Version Of Configuration and select **Service**.
  - In the Containment tree, select a Service and drag it onto the Version of Configuration. (In this case, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens in the second step.) The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Assignment Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

The result of this procedure is as the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Removing Service from a Services Roadmap diagram

To remove a Service

1. Right-click the Service bar, and from the shortcut menu, select Remove > Service <name of the resource you want to remove>.

##### Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram

To delete a Version of Configuration

1. Select a Version of Configuration.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
