(In this case, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Assignment Wizard" /></ac:link> opens in the second step.)<br /></span></span></span>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Service Assignment Wizard" /></ac:link> opens.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The result of this procedure is as the following figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="SV-8_version_of_configuration.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Removing Service from a Services Roadmap diagram</h3><p>To remove a Service</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Service bar, and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Remove </strong>&gt;<strong> Service </strong>&lt;name of the resource you want to remove&gt;.</li></ol><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Removing Version of Configurations from a Services Roadmap diagram</h3><p>To delete a Version of Configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Version of Configuration.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966222 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06781: Working with standard profiles

- page_id: `243966222`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966222/Working+with+standard+profiles
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2026-06-18T10:19:36.980+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All profiles and libraries that are bundled with the modeling tool are considered as standard/system resources, non-modifiable and essential for the proper performance of the tool. 
 
We highly recommend not to modify our provided standard profiles and libraries as it could cause problems on version updates, plugins, core malfunctions, and model corruptions.

Users will be warned on any intentional or unintentional attempt to modify profiles when:

- Opening profiles as projects.
- Using profiles in the read-write mode.
- Importing profiles into a project.
- Merging projects, and several other occasions.

##### Plugin and Profile Versions

Standard profiles are usually upgraded to support the latest versions of the specification of standards they represent (for example, SysML 1.2 or UML 2.4) in every modeling tool release cycle. The modeling tool application, plugins code and behavior are modified accordingly to reflect these changes.

There is a high probability that the latest version of a modeling tool or plugin won't work with earlier or later profile versions and may cause unpredictable behavior or even model distortions. For example, SysML plugin 16.5 requires SysML Profile 16.5, as it could malfunction when using SysML Profile 16.0 or 16.6.

To protect users from such inconveniences, every project stores information about profiles or plugins versions that were used to create it and are required to load data correctly. Every standard profile has a version number. Normally, it's the same as modeling tool (or plugin) version number.

The modeling tool requires using corresponding profile and software versions. If you use plugins or profiles that are obsolete, you will see a warning message. If you are missing certain plugins or profiles, Resource Manager with selected resources to install is launched automatically, so you can install missing resources in just few clicks.

If new versions of plugins are not purchased or you simply don't want to install it, but need to take a look at the project content, warnings may be ignored and such project may be loaded. In this case, proxy elements for missing profile elements will be created to retain missing references. Missing custom diagrams will be loaded as regular UML diagrams or will be restricted for review.

Do not save such projects! Use them for preview only.

The modeling tool is forward-compatible; old projects will be loaded/converted without any warnings if you have the latest versions of the corresponding plugins and profiles.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>All profiles and libraries that are bundled with the modeling tool are considered as standard/system resources, non-modifiable and essential for the proper performance of the tool. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />We highly recommend not to modify our provided standard profiles and libraries as it could cause problems on version updates, plugins, core malfunctions, and model corruptions.</p><p>Users will be warned on any intentional or unintentional attempt to modify profiles when:</p><ul><li>Opening profiles as projects.</li><li>Using profiles in the read-write mode.</li><li>Importing profiles into a project.</li><li>Merging projects, and several other occasions.</li></ul><h3>Plugin and Profile Versions</h3><p>Standard profiles are usually upgraded to support the latest versions of the specification of standards they represent (for example, SysML 1.2 or UML 2.4) in every modeling tool release cycle. The modeling tool application, plugins code and behavior are modified accordingly to reflect these changes.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />There is a high probability that the latest version of a modeling tool or plugin won't work with earlier or later profile versions and may cause unpredictable behavior or even model distortions. For example, SysML plugin 16.5 requires SysML Profile 16.5, as it could malfunction when using SysML Profile 16.0 or 16.6.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To protect users from such inconveniences, every project stores information about profiles or plugins versions that were used to create it and are required to load data correctly. Every standard profile has a version number. Normally, it's the same as modeling tool (or plugin) version number.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />The modeling tool requires using corresponding profile and software versions. If you use plugins or profiles that are obsolete, you will see a warning message. If you are missing certain plugins or profiles, Resource Manager with selected resources to install is launched automatically, so you can install missing resources in just few clicks.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />If new versions of plugins are not purchased or you simply don't want to install it, but need to take a look at the project content, warnings may be ignored and such project may be loaded. In this case, proxy elements for missing profile elements will be created to retain missing references. Missing custom diagrams will be loaded as regular UML diagrams or will be restricted for review.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f8f0208-aa39-442c-aefd-eb8701f3b275"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Do not save such projects! Use them for preview only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The modeling tool is forward-compatible; old projects will be loaded/converted without any warnings if you have the latest versions of the corresponding plugins and profiles.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Auto updated profiles.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564734 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06782: Working with Standards Roadmap table

- page_id: `249564734`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564734/Working+with+Standards+Roadmap+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:18.803+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Standards > Standards Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1658889191

1658889203

1658889192

To create a Standards Roadmap table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
2. Follow the steps of the wizard.

When the table is created, you can later add new or existing resources as well as add or remove columns.

##### Adding rows

To add rows with new resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add New .
2. Choose a resource type from the list.
3. In the opened Select Owner dialog, choose the owner.
4. In the Specification dialog, type the name for the new resource.

To add rows with existing resources

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the opened Select Element dialog, choose a needed resource.
3. Click OK .

##### Adding new columns

To add new columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose one or more needed Actual Strategic Phases.
3. Click OK .

##### Filling cells

When you add rows and columns, you can begin filling out the cells by adding Subjects of Forecast.

If you add the resources which are connected through the Forecast relationship, the Subject of Forecast is added automatically after creating a table.

To add a Subject of Forecast

1. Double-click a needed cell.
2. Click the ... button.
3. In the **Select Subject of Forecast** dialog, choose the resources you need to add. You can also create resources using the Creation Mode.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

When you add the resources into cells, the Forecast relationship is created between the added resource and the resource which represents the row. The relationship's Start and End dates are filled according to the dates defined in the relevant Actual Strategic Phase.

##### Removing columns

To remove columns

1. In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Add / Remove Columns .
2. In the opened Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, remove Actual Strategic Phases from selection or click Remove All. [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_columns.png']
3. Click OK .

##### Sorting columns

After you add new columns, you can sort them according to Actual Strategic Phase dates.

To sort columns

- In the Resources Forecast table toolbar, click Sort Columns .

1658889189

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resources Forecast Creation Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564740 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06783: Working with Standards Structure diagram

- page_id: `249564740`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564740/Working+with+Standards+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:45.108+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Standards > Standards Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1233356123

1233356126

1233356121

**On this page**

1233356125

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Standards Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Standards Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Standards Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Standards Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Protocol element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Protocol in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Protocol and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Protocol in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Standards Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Protocol and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_protocol.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Protocol (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Protocols

When you have Protocol created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Protocol Layers (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Association** and choose **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.change_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.display_related.png']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='standards_comparments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
  - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='standards.str.display_related_nested.png']

1233362204

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='']

Composite element and note the Protocol Layers (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="281" ac:width="352"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.information_in_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Association</strong> and choose <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="222" ac:width="319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_select_aggregation.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="201" ac:width="315"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.change_role_name.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="667" ac:width="315"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.change_multiplicity.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8531680-d4c7-4ff0-ac31-c8bbb22dad98"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="856" ac:width="501"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.display_related.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_comparments.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="47006c6c-2346-4e29-a79c-0528261134c2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="761" ac:width="696"><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards.str.display_related_nested.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e296adc4-a21b-4a67-8ced-d13a01ff9aa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1233362204</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564758 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06784: Working with Standards Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249564758`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564758/Working+with+Standards+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:45.607+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Standards > Standards Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1424709417

1424709419

1424709416

**On this page**

1424709418

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Standards Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Standards Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Standards Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_standards_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Standards Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Standard element, but the same is valid for other elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Standard (or other appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_standard.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Standards Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Standard (or other appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Standard (or other appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have Standard (or other appropriate element) created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the diagram palette, choose Generalization.
3. Connect the Standard elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] [IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564769 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06785: Working with Standards Taxonomy Table

- page_id: `249564769`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564769/Working+with+Standards+Taxonomy+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:24.994+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Standards > Standards Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Standards Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button to add a Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Standard dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Standards Taxonomy table</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to add a Protocol, Protocol Stack, or Standard. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog, select the desired elements. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564774 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06786: Working with Standards Traceability table

- page_id: `249564774`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564774/Working+with+Standards+Traceability+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:25.185+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Standards > Standards Traceability
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Standards Traceability table

- Create a new element: 1) Click the Add New button. 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element. 3) In the Select Owner dialog choose the owner and click OK . 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click Close . The element appears under the Resource element column. 5) Select the cell under the Standard/Policy column and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 6) In the Select Standard dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.
- Add an existing element. 
 1) Click the**Add Existing** button. 
 2) In the **Select UAF element** dialog select desired elements and click **OK**. The element appears under the **Resource element** column. 
 3) Select the cell under the **Standard/Policy** column and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . 
 4) In the **Select Standard** dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Standards Traceability table</p><hr /><ul><li>Create a new element:<br /> 1) Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button.<br /> 2) From the drop-down list select the desired element.<br /> 3) In the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog choose the owner and click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /> 4) In the element's Specification window type its name and click <strong>Close</strong>. The element appears under the <strong>Resource element</strong> column.<br /> 5) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image></span> .<br /> 6) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.<span> </span></li><li><p>Add an existing element.<br /> 1) Click the<strong> Add Existing</strong> button.<br /> 2) In the <strong>Select UAF element</strong> dialog select desired elements and click <strong>OK</strong>. The element appears under the <strong>Resource element</strong> column.<br /> 3) Select the cell under the <strong>Standard/Policy</strong> column and click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image></span> .<br /> 4) In the <strong>Select Standard</strong> dialog select one or more Standards, Protocols, or Protocol Stacks.</p></li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966419 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06787: Working with State Machine diagram

- page_id: `243966419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966419/Working+with+State+Machine+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:00.089+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams > State Machine diagram
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Displaying inner elements

To display inner elements

- When creating a new State Machine diagram, right-click a State Machine element in the Containment tree, select **Create Diagram**, and then click **State****Machine****Diagram**. Inner elements are displayed on the State Machine diagram pane automatically upon creating the first diagram under the State Machine element.
- When modifying a preexisting State Machine diagram, select the diagram pane or the State shape and perform one of the following steps: - From the shortcut menu, click Display > Display Inner Elements . - On the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select Display Inner Elements .

##### Working with behaviors

You can select Entry, Do Activity, Exit, deferrable triggers, and internal transitions directly in the compartment of the State shape. Thus, you can:

- Draw dependencies among particular behaviors on a state shape.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dragging objects' space=''] from one state shape to another.
- Convert the selected behavior to another.

To convert a behavior to another behavior

1. On a State shape, select a behavior.
2. From the behavior's shortcut menu, select Refactor > Convert To > < behavior >.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Displaying inner elements</h3><p>To display inner elements</p><hr /><ul><li><p>When creating a new State Machine diagram, right-click a State Machine element in the Containment tree, select <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, and then click <strong>State </strong><strong>Machine </strong><strong>Diagram</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b83bd2e-f095-401f-a367-fd110427b8fe"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Inner elements are displayed on the State Machine diagram pane automatically upon creating the first diagram under the State Machine element.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>When modifying a preexisting State Machine diagram, select the diagram pane or the State shape and perform one of the following steps:<br />      - From the shortcut menu, click <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Display Inner Elements</strong>.<br />      - On the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and select<strong> Display Inner Elements</strong>.</li></ul><h3>Working with behaviors</h3><p>You can select Entry, Do Activity, Exit, deferrable triggers, and internal transitions directly in the compartment of the State shape. Thus, you can:</p><ul><li>Draw dependencies among particular behaviors on a state shape.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Dragging objects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Move behaviors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> from one state shape to another.</li><li>Convert the selected behavior to another.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To convert a behavior to another behavior</p><hr /><ol><li>On a State shape, select a behavior.</li><li>From the behavior's shortcut menu, select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Convert To</strong> &gt; &lt;<em>behavior</em>&gt;.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563919 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06788: Working with Strategic Actual Deployment diagram

- page_id: `249563919`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563919/Working+with+Strategic+Actual+Deployment+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:28.150+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

923113496

923113498

923113495

**On this page**

923113497

##### Creating Strategic Actual Deployment table

The context of the Strategic Actual Deployment table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Structure' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the Strategic Actual Deployment table is Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the Strategic Actual Deployment table are Actual Responsible Resources, and the columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a StrategicActual Deployment table

To create an empty Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a Strategic Roadmap package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > Strategic Actual Deployment .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > Strategic Actual Deployment .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

To remove Capabilities from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Phases relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

To remove the deployment of the Actual Resources from a Strategic Actual Deployment table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-**button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Actual Deployment table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

228689575

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09cf68f8-b16c-4993-954c-bb166c52ec57"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923113496</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09b92ce0-c4e4-4ba6-b7e4-65c7b4fdb33d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923113498</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6a33d4c-f1e9-44eb-bf49-539d425e0429"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923113495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e103f8cc-f6bb-4352-81e7-193197a28738" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0036c664-e3b6-44bc-ae30-7ffdd4469a66"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">923113497</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating Strategic Actual Deployment table</h3><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fd3543ca-7435-4e44-8a52-f15595d28745"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the Strategic Actual Deployment table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Structure" /></ac:link> <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1f2ccbe4-9e4a-4cc7-994f-ba3df0e1d00e">view</ac:inline-comment-marker>.</span></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fd3543ca-7435-4e44-8a52-f15595d28745"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_structure_relation_to_deployment.png" /></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="fd3543ca-7435-4e44-8a52-f15595d28745">The content of the Strateg</ac:inline-comment-marker>ic Actual Deployment table is Actual R<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="4a2a74e9-d256-4d11-a669-0d5ab6451737">esources</ac:inline-comment-marker> use<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="162b09a0-32c0-4089-913f-82eedf6ff08f">d by Actual Re</ac:inline-comment-marker>sponsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e0209b00-cc98-4150-af91-89e4c96e7b9f">to realize Capabilities in a</ac:inline-comment-marker> particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the Strategic Actual Deployment table are Actual Responsible Resources, and the columns are Capabilities.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5124318-7a44-4b50-acc7-4bc4bfa6ad14"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.</p><p>There can be one or more Actual R<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="358ca12c-1c73-454f-9d37-e0f34c1d1b66">esources</ac:inline-comment-marker> related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual R<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6889e688-0aa4-44dd-90fd-a450765cb1bc">esource</ac:inline-comment-marker> is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual R<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c38f9f88-e9a7-45cd-9c56-57e0c706124a">esource</ac:inline-comment-marker> is no longer <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8927b3b9-bead-42b1-ba01-c4c90abe1632">used</ac:inline-comment-marker>. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p>Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the Strategic Actual Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a Strategic Actual Deployment table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a Strategic </span>Actual Deployment table</h4><p>To create an empty Strategic Actual Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a Strategic Roadmap package as the diagram owner and select <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563984 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06789: Working with Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table

- page_id: `249563984`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563984/Working+with+Strategic+Actual+Strategic+Phase+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:04.521+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**The Strategic Actual Strategic Phase Table (St-Tx)** specifies all the Actual Strategic Phases referenced throughout one or more architectures. The view can also be used as a source document to develop high-level use cases and user requirements.

When the table display mode is set to a **List**, adding a new or existing Actual Strategic Phase adds a new row at the end of the table.

To add a new Actual Strategic Phase

1. Click the Add New button and choose one of the following:
  - Actual Enduring Task
  - Actual Enterprise Phase
  - Enterprise Mission
  - Value Stream
  - Whole Life Enterprise
2. (Optional) In the Select Strategic Phase dialog, select an existing Strategic Phase or create a new one to be assigned as an Actual Strategic Phase type.
3. Click OK .

To add an existingActual Strategic Phase

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, select the existing Actual Strategic Phase.

When you add new or existing Actual Strategic Phases when the table display mode is a **Compact**or **Complete**tree, the Actual Strategic Phases are added according to a predefined structure of Strategic Phases.

To add a new Actual Strategic Phase

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Add New button.
  - Select an Actual Strategic Phase with a defined Strategic Phase structure and click the Add New button.
2. In the Select Strategic Phase dialog, choose an existing Strategic Phase or create a new one.
3. Click OK .

After you click **OK**, the new row will be added either at the bottom of the table or under the Actual Strategi Phase if you have chosen it.

To add an existing Actual Strategic Phase

1. Do one of the following:
  1. Click the Add Existing button.
  2. Select an Actual Strategic Phase with a defined Strategic Phase structure and click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Strategic Phase dialog, choose an existing Actual Strategic Phase or create a new one.
3. Click **OK**.

After you click **OK**, the new row will be added either at the bottom of the table or under the Actual Strategic Phase if you have chosen it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d17632c4-181e-43f3-80c4-1e5bd168b354">Add New</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker a new one.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>After you click <strong>OK</strong>, the new row will be added either at the bottom of the table or under the Actual Strategi Phase if you chosen it.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_existing_defined_structure_exampla.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0e8b2050-c700-4b89-a4ca-7d3ab9fd207b">display mode</ac:inline-comment-marker> by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_actual_strategic_phase_taxonomy_table_views.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563884 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06790: Working with Strategic Connectivity diagram

- page_id: `249563884`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563884/Working+with+Strategic+Connectivity+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:26.157+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. Create or reuse Capabilities from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Taxonomy' space=''] (recommended).
2. Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Connectivity and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Connectivity . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_connectivity_diagram.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic **Connectivity** and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Actual Enterprise Phase element, but the same is valid for other Actual Strategic Phase elements.

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Actual Enterprise Phase and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability_palette.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Actual Enterprise Phase and select it. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability_containment_tree.png']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='drag_capability_to_diagram.png']

The structure of each Capability can be modeled using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram' space=''].

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose an appropriate element.

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [Creating elements from other resources](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+elements+from+other+resources).

##### Creating Strategic Exchanges

Before creating Strategic Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Resource Performer, Strategic Information, Geo Politic Extent Type or their specializations). The you can start creating Strategic Exchanges.

To create a Strategic Exchange in the Strategic Connectivity diagram

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram pane, select an Actual Enterprise Phase and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Strategic Exchange. Click another Actual Enterprise Phase to connect. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_exchange_sm.png']
  - In the diagram palette, c lick **Strategic Exchange** and on the diagram pane, connect Actual Enterprise Phases. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_exchange_pallete.png'] The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Strategic Exchange** wizard opens.
2. Specify the Exchange Name, Conveyed Item, and Direction. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Strategic Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Strategic Exchange on the diagram pane. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Strategic Exchange wizard' space='']**Realized Strategic Exchange**wizard opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a74932b-5953-498b-a2ec-0b27e12fccbe" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create or reuse Capabilities from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Taxonomy" /></ac:link> (recommended).</li><li>Connect the Capabilities using Dependencies.</li></ol><h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p>To create a Strategic Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Connectivity and do one of the following:<ul><li>From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Strategic Connectivity</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="206" ac:width="325"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_connectivity_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Strategic <strong>Connectivity </strong></strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p>When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Actual Enterprise Phase element, but the same is valid for other Actual Strategic Phase elements.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the Actual Enterprise Phase and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_capability_palette.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Name the element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create an Actual Enterprise Phase in a Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the Actual Enterprise Phase and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_capability_containment_tree.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_capability_to_diagram.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br />The structure of each Capability can be modeled using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Taxonomy diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose an appropriate element.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+elements+from+other+resources">Creating elements from other resources</a>.</p><h3>Creating Strategic Exchanges</h3><p>Before creating Strategic Exchanges, one or more exchange items should be created (e.g. Resource Performer, Strategic Information, Geo Politic Extent Type or their specializations). The you can start creating Strategic Exchanges.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Strategic Exchange in the Strategic Connectivity diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the diagram pane, select an Actual Enterprise Phase and in the smart manipulator toolbar, click Strategic Exchange. Click another Actual Enterprise Phase to connect.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_exchange_sm.png" /></ac:image></li><li> In the diagram palette, c<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">lick <strong>Strategic Exchange</strong> and on the diagram pane, connect Actual Enterprise Phases.</span> <br /></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_exchange_pallete.png" /></ac:image><br /><br />The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Strategic Exchange</strong> wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the Exchange Name, Conveyed Item, and Direction.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="realized_strategic_exchange_wizard.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Click<strong> Finish </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you did not specify the Conveyed Item while creating the Strategic Exchange, you can set it later, directly in the diagram picking the needed element from the diagram palette.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.</li><li>Drag the selected Conveyed Item on to the needed Strategic Exchange on the diagram pane. The<span> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Realized Strategic Exchange wizard" /><ac:link-body><strong>Realized Strategic Exchange</strong><span> </span>wizard</ac:link-body></ac:link><span> </span>opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click<span> </span><strong>Finish<span> </span></strong>when you are done. </li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_conveyed_item_palette_strategic.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563894 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06791: Working with Strategic Connectivity matrix

- page_id: `249563894`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563894/Working+with+Strategic+Connectivity+matrix
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:26.570+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Connectivity
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1283082281

1283082283

1283082282

The Strategic Connectivity Matrix (St-Cn) shows the direct and indirect (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']) dependencies between the Capabilities.

The Rows and the Columns of this matrix are Capabilities.

To build the matrix

1. Specify Rows scope (Capabilities).
2. Specify Columns scope (Capabilities).
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.

Capabilities map to Capabilities using Dependency relationship.

To map Capability to Capability

- Double-click the intersection between the desired elements.
- Right-click the intersection between the desired elements and select Implements.

1283082280

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with matrices' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563900 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06792: Working with Strategic Constraints Definition diagram

- page_id: `249563900`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563900/Working+with+Strategic+Constraints+Definition+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:26.859+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Strategic Constraints Definition diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Constraints package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Constraints Definition . [ATTACHMENT filename='new_strategic_constraints_definition.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic Constraints Definition and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='new_capability_for_strategic_constraints.png']
2. Name the element.

To create an element in the Containment tree

1. Right-click the Strategic Constraints package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Search for the Capability (or another appropriate element) and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Security Enclave (or another appropriate element).

##### Creating and applying Strategic Constraints

You can create the Strategic Constraints in the same way as other elements - from the diagram palette or in the Containment tree. See . Also, you can create constraints in the selected Capability Specification window.

To create a Strategic Constraint in the Specification window

1. Select a needed Capability a nd open its Specification window.
2. In the Constraints property group, click Create > Strategic > Strategic Constraint .
3. Specify the name.
4. In the Specification cell, define the rule. The specification(rule) can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

Capability is a constrained element that can be a subject of the Strategic Constraint.

To apply Strategic Constraints, do one of the following

- In the Containment tree, select a Strategic Constraint and drag it on the Capability .
- In the needed Capability Specification window, go to Constraints property group. Click the Apply button and from the Select Element window, choose a constraint.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

Click the <strong>Apply </strong>button and from the <strong>Select Element</strong> window, choose a constraint.</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563906 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06793: Working with Strategic Constraints table

- page_id: `249563906`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563906/Working+with+Strategic+Constraints+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:02.384+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The**Strategic Constraints**table specifies constraints that apply to capabilities.

To create a Strategic Constraints table

1. Click the Add New button. The new row is added.
2. In the Applies To cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the**Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint**dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.
3. Click **OK**.
4. In the**Rule****Specification** cell, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Specification** dialog, select the language and specify the body. The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).

To add an existing Strategic Constraint

1. Click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Elements dialog, select Strategic Constraints. The filled row is added to the table.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Constraints table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The new row is added.</span></li><li>In the<strong> Applies To</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>and in the<span> </span><strong>Select Subject Of Strategic Constraint</strong><span> </span>dialog, choose the needed constrained Capabilities.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">In the<strong> Rule </strong><strong>Specification</strong> cell, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>and in</span> the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog, select the language and specify the body.<br /></span></span></p><p>The Rule Specification can be defined in English as well as using expressions or formulas (OCL).</p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></span></p><p>To add an existing Strategic Constraint</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button. </li><li>In the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog, select Strategic Constraints. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563993 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06794: Working with Strategic Drivers Table

- page_id: `249563993`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563993/Working+with+Strategic+Drivers+Table
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:31.036+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Motivation
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

4

**The Strategic Drivers Table (St-Mv)**allows you to assemble Drivers, Challenges, Opportunities, and Capabilities in a tabular form. Also, this table helps define relationships between Drivers and Challenges, Challenges and Opportunities, Opportunities and Capabilities. This table captures the Driver, Challenge, Opportunity, and Capability elements in strict order in each row: the Driver presenting Challenges, the Challenges motivating Opportunities, and the Opportunities impacting Capabilities.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Strategic Drivers Table

##### Creating Strategic Drivers Table diagram

When you choose to create Strategic Drivers Table, the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard' space='']** opens.

To create a Strategic Drivers Table from the wizard

1. Right-click the Strategic Motivation Package and select Create Diagram > Strategic Drivers Table .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard' space=''] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Strategic Drivers Table** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show Strategic Drivers Creation Wizard property value to true .

To create a Strategic Drivers Table when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard' space='']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Strategic Motivation Package and select Create Diagram > Strategic Drivers Table .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Strategic Drivers Table chart is created.

##### Working with table

###### Choosing display mode

You can choose to include the element from the entire model as addition to those added or created from the Strategic Drivers Table. To accomplish this, in the table Criteria area, select the **Include All Related Elements from Model** check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of how the table content changes when the**Include All Related Elements from Model**check box is selected.

###### Adding Drivers

To add a new Driver

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add New button.
2. In the open Select Owner dialog, choose the owner for the Driver you are creating.
3. Click OK .

The new Driver is added to the table as a row.

To add an existing Driver

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the open Select Owner dialog, choose one or more Drivers.
3. Click OK.

The existing Driver is added to the table as a row.

You can also add new elements to the table by dragging and dropping them from the Containment tree to the table.

###### Removing Drivers

To remove an elementfrom the chart

1. Select an element.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Table button.

To delete an element from the model

1. Select an element.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

###### Managing Challenges

To add new Challenges

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_drivers_table_edit.png']
2. In the open Select Challenge dialog, click the Creation Mode button.
3. Choose the owner for a new Challenge.
4. Click the Create button, and choose Strategic > Challenge.
5. Fill out needed information and click **Close**.
6. Click OK .

To add existing Challenges

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_drivers_table_edit.png']
2. In the open Select Challenge dialog, select needed Challenges from the list and added them su the Selected elements list.
3. Click OK .

You can also add new elements or replace existing ones in the cell by dragging and dropping them from the Containment tree to the table cell. If you want to add more Challenges to the particular cell, choose the needed Challenges in the Containment tree, press Ctrl and drag them to the needed cell.

When you add Challenge elements into cells, the**Presented By**relationship is created between**Challenge**and Driver elements if the needed relationships do not exist in the Model.

In case of new relationship creation, the background action is logged and the the notification will be displayed in the **Notification Window** informing you about the newly created relationships. Learn more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space=''].

###### Managing Opportunities

Adding new Opportunities

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. The Manage Opportunities and Motivated By relationships dialog opens.
2. In t he **Manage Opportunities and Motivated By relationships**dialog , click the **Edit**button.
3. In the open **Select Opportunity** dialog, click **Creation Mode**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Choose an appropriate owner for a new Opportunity.
5. Click **Create** > **Strategic**> **Opportunity**.
6. Enter specifications for the Opportunity.
7. Click **Close**. A new Opportunity is added to the **Selected elements** list.

Add existing Opportunities

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. The Manage Opportunities and Motivated By relationships dialog opens.
2. In t he **Manage Opportunities and Motivated By relationships**dialog , click the **Edit**button.
3. Choose one or more Opportunities and click the Add button (or double-click Opportunity to add it). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also add new elements or replace existing ones in the cell by dragging and dropping them from the Containment tree to the table cell. If you want to add more Opportunitiesto the particular cell, choose the needed Opportunitiesin the Containment tree, press Ctrl and drag them to the needed cell.

To map the Challenges with Opportunities

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. The Manage Opportunities and Motivated By relationships dialog opens.
2. In the open dialog, drag a selected Challenge onto the needed Opportunity to create a Motivated By relationship.
3. Click OK .

mng_opWhen you add Opportunity elements into cells, the **Motivated****By** relationship is created between **Opportunity**and Challenge elements if the needed relationships do not exist in the Model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the **Manage Opportunities and Motivated By relationships**dialog.

In case of new relationship creation, the background action is logged and the the notification will be displayed in the **Notification Window** informing you about the newly created relationships. Learn more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space=''].

To remove the element mapping

- In t he Manage Opportunities and Motivated By relationships dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a relation and click the Delete button.
  - Click the Delete All button to remove all mapping relations.

###### Managing Capabilities

Adding new Capabilities

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. The Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships dialog opens.
2. In t he **Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships**dialog , click the **Edit**button.
3. In the open **Select Capability**dialog, click **Creation Mode**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Choose an appropriate owner for a new Capability.
5. Click **Create** > **Strategic**> **Capability**.
6. Enter specifications for the Capability.
7. Click **Close**. A new Capability is added to the **Selected elements** list.

Add existing Capabilities

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. The Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships dialog opens.
2. In t he Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships dialog, click the **Edit**button.
3. Choose one or more Capabilities and click the Add button (or double-click Capability to add it). 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can also add new elements or replace existing ones in the cell by dragging and dropping them from the Containment tree to the table cell. If you want to add more Capabilitiesto the particular cell, choose the needed Capabilitiesin the Containment tree, press Ctrl and drag them to the needed cell.

To map the Opportunities with Capabilities

1. In the table, double-click the needed cell and then click the Edit button. The Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships dialog opens.
2. In the open dialog, drag a selected Opportunity onto the needed Capability to create an Impacted By relationship.
3. Click OK .

mng_capWhen you add Capability elements into cells, the **Impacted****By** relationship is created between **Capability**and Opportunity elements if the needed relationships do not exist in the Model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the **Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships**dialog.

In case of new relationship creation, the background action is logged and the the notification will be displayed in the **Notification Window** informing you about the newly created relationships. Learn more about [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space=''].

To remove the element mapping

- In t he Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a relation and click the Delete button.
  - Click the Delete All button to remove all mapping relations.

##### Generating Reports

The Strategic Driver Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:

- Directly from the table toolbar
- Using a quick report option from the Containment tree
- Through the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of a Strategic Drivers Table report

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
