Also, this table helps define relationships between Drivers and Challenges, Challenges and Opportunities, Opportunities and Capabilities. This table captures the Driver, Challenge, Opportunity, and Capability elements in strict order in each row: the Driver presenting Challenges, the Challenges motivating Opportunities, and the Opportunities impacting Capabilities.</span></p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_driver_table.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of the Strategic Drivers Table</span></h6><h3>Creating Strategic Drivers Table diagram</h3><p>When you choose to create Strategic Drivers Table, the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a Strategic Drivers Table from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Strategic Motivation Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Strategic Drivers Table</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong><strong>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e743b31-08c5-4ba8-8111-15b730e83500"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Strategic Drivers Table</strong> check box.</p><p>To restore the usage of the<strong> Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; The <strong>Manage Capabilities and Impacted By</strong> <strong>relationships </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the open dialog, drag a selected Opportunity onto the needed Capability to create an Impacted By relationship.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4718569-9f00-49f8-b41b-d447297cb099"><ac:parameter ac:name="">mng_cap</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>When you add Capability elements into cells, the <strong>Impacted </strong></span><strong style="text-align: left;">By</strong> relationship is created between <strong>Capability </strong>and Opportunity elements if the needed relationships do not exist in the Model.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="manage_capabilities_and_impacted_by_relationships_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">An example of the <strong>Manage Capabilities and Impacted By relationships</strong><span> dialog.</span></h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5212769e-244b-4074-b892-2cf1038b1003"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In case of new relationship creation, the background action is logged and the the notification will be displayed in the <strong>Notification Window</strong> informing you about the newly created relationships. Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /></ac:link>. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To remove the element mapping</p><hr /><ul><li><span>In t</span><span>he </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Manage Capabilities and Impacted By</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">relationships </strong>dialog, do one of the following:<ul><li>Select a relation and click the <strong>Delete </strong>button.</li><li>Click the <strong>Delete All</strong> button to remove all mapping relations.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Generating Reports</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">The Strategic Driver Table has a predefined report available. You can generate the report:</span></p><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Directly from the table toolbar</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Using a quick report option from the Containment tree</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Through the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_driver_table_report.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">An example of a Strategic Drivers Table report</span></h6></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Drivers Table Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564005 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06796: Working with Strategic Motivation diagram

- page_id: `249564005`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564005/Working+with+Strategic+Motivation+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:29.585+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Motivation
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Strategic Motivation diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Motivation and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Motivation . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_motivation.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic Motivation and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. To illustrate the example, Opportunity and Challenge elements are used, but you can also choose Driver or Effect.

To create an Opportunity in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click Opportunity and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.
2. Name the element.

To create an Opportunity in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Strategic Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Select Opportunity .
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability.

##### Connect the elements

When you have Opportunity created, you can start connecting them using the Enables or Motivated By relationships. If you choose Enables relationship, the Enterprise Goal is created on the other end of the relationship. If you choose Motivated By, a Challenge is created on the other end of the relationship.

To connect the elements with the Motivated By relationship

1. Select an element from which the Motivates By will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Motivated By. You can also select the Motivated By relationship from the diagram palette.
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create an Strategic Motivation diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Motivation and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Strategic Motivation</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="375" ac:width="331"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_motivation.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Strategic Motivation </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. To illustrate the example, Opportunity and Challenge elements are used, but you can also choose  Driver or Effect.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an Opportunity in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click Opportunity and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.<br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Opportunity in the Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Right click the Strategic Motivation package and from the shortcut menu, select </span><strong>Create Element</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. Select Opportunity</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">.<br /></span></li><li>Name the element.</li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose Capability.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have Opportunity created, you can start connecting them using the Enables or Motivated By relationships. If you choose Enables relationship, the Enterprise Goal is created on the other end of the relationship. If you choose Motivated By, a Challenge is created on the other end of the relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Motivated By relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element from which the Motivates By will be drawn.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Motivated By.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc7b57ae-a1f8-48e0-811e-28d2602359f3"><ac:rich-text-body>You can also select the Motivated By relationship from the diagram palette.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.</span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564008 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06797: Working with Strategic Motivation Table

- page_id: `249564008`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564008/Working+with+Strategic+Motivation+Table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:38:51.828+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Motivation
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A**Strategic Motivation Table (St-Mv)** allows you to organize Strategic requirements (Enterprise Goals, Enterprise Objectives) in a tabular form. Because the requirements are text-based, using a table format for inputting requirement details is more convenient. This format resembles a spreadsheet, offering more space than the confined boxes in a diagram. In this table, each row corresponds to a requirement, while the columns capture various properties of each requirement. With this table, you can:

- Create new requirements directly in the table or import the existing ones from your model.
- Directly edit the properties of the requirements.
- Directly generate requirement reports, renumber the requirement IDs, or export the table to CSV or HTML format files.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Strategic Motivation Table.

To create a new requirement in the table

1. In the table toolbar, click Add New . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_motivation_add_new.png']
2. From the menu, choose the needed requirement type.
3. Add the requirement's name and text.

To create a new nested requirement

1. In the table, select a requirement for which you want to create a nested requirement.
2. In the table toolbar, click Add Nested . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_motivation_add_nested.png']
3. From the menu, choose the needed requirement type.
4. Add the requirement's name and text.

To add an existing Requirement to the table

1. In the table toolbar, click Add Existing .
2. In the **Select Requirement** dialog, choose the requirement you need. If you want to add more than one existing requirement, in the **Select Requirement** dialog, click **Multiple Selection**.
3. Click OK .

This table is based on the Requirement table. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Requirement Table' space='']>>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Because the requirements are text-based, using a table format for inputting requirement details is more convenient. This format resembles a spreadsheet, offering more space than the confined boxes in a diagram. In this table, each row corresponds to a requirement, while the columns capture various properties of each requirement. With this table, you can:</p><ul><li>Create new requirements directly in the table or import the existing ones from your model.</li><li>Directly edit the properties of the an existing Requirement to the table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Add Existing</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Requirement</strong> dialog, choose the requirement you need.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7d90d594-141c-446a-9652-aa753ccb43d1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to add more <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563911 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06798: Working with Strategic Parametric diagram

- page_id: `249563911`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563911/Working+with+Strategic+Parametric+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:27.414+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Parameters
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1992025934

1992025954

1992025944

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Capability. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

1992025923

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="309b9b67-1e24-40fa-9243-c4dfb2fe59f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1992025923</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563923 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06799: Working with Strategic Phasing diagram

- page_id: `249563923`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563923/Working+with+Strategic+Phasing+diagram
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:28.570+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating Strategic Phasing diagram

When you choose to create Strategic Phasing diagram, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** opens.

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space=''] chapter.

To create a Strategic Phasing diagram from the wizard

1. Right-click the Strategic Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Strategic Phasing .
2. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space=''].
3. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create Strategic Phasing** check box.

To create an Strategic Phasing diagram manually when **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']** is disabled

1. Right-click the Strategic Roadmap Package and select Create Diagram > Strategic Phasing .
2. Type the diagram name.
3. Press Enter. The blank Strategic Phasing chart is created.

To restore the usage of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram Creation Wizard' space='']**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart Creation Wizard property value to true .

##### Adding Capabilities

To add a new Capability

- On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Capability . An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done

##### Adding Sub Capabilities

To add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

To add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Capability Provisions

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

The capability name becomes [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space=''] when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Deleting Capabilities and Sub Capabilities

To remove a Capability or Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability or Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability or Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Capabilityor Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Showing or hiding Capability Provisions area

You can choose whether you want to show or hide selected Capability's Provisions in the Capability Provisions area in the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart.

To show or hide Capability Provisions

1. In the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart, select a Capability.
2. On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click the Show/Hide Capability Provisions Area button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The bars in the Capability Provisions Area are displayed in colors according to the viewpoints.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision that you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision that you want to remove from the chart.

##### Aligning Bar Positions

You can adjust the visible part of the Gantt chart bars. You can either shift the bars to the left based on the selected bar's start date and marking options, or keep the default visibility, determined by the earliest bar date and marking options.

In the image below, the bars were moved to left to align with a selected *Financial Planning*bar.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Show/hide the sub-resources in Capability Provisions panel

You can show or hide the sub-resources in the Capability provision panel. The Show/Hide Sub Resources in the Capability Provision area button alows you to change the sub-resources visibility.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adding, editing, and removing In Service and Out of Service dates

When the sub-resources are shown, you can add, edit, or remove the In Service and Out of Service dates for the sub-resources directly in the Capability Provision area.

To add the In Service / Out of Service dates

1. In the Capability Provision area, right-click the needed sub-resource.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Add In Service / Out of Service command. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
3. Follow the steps of the wizard.
4. Click Finish .

To edit the In Service / Out of Service dates

1. In the Capability Provision area, right-click the needed sub-resource.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Edit In Service / Out of Service command. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
3. Modify the needed dates.
4. Click **Finish**.

To remove the In Service / Out of Service dates

1. In the Capability Provision area, right-click the needed sub-resource.
2. In the shortcut menu, click Remove In Service / Out of Service command.

##### Set the sub-resources depth in Capability Provisions panel

You can control the number of sub-resources displayed in the Capability Provisions panel. The **Sub-resources Depth in Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart** project option lets you define the hierarchy level of sub-resources shown in the Capability Provisions panel across the Strategic Phasing, CV-3, StV-3, NCV-3, and Cr Gantt Charts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering table data' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e35916f-6e2b-4a09-995e-c7baf64a71fa" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Creating Strategic Phasing diagram</h3><p>When you choose to create Strategic Phasing diagram, <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Phasing Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> opens.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95a283d7-ac16-44af-bffb-bfda138b8c03"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link> chapter.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create a Strategic Phasing diagram from the wizard</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Strategic Roadmap Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Strategic Phasing</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Phasing Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</strong></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1ccb972-4d50-438b-9600-348f9c84a65a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not wish to use the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Phasing Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> next time, clear the <strong>Show the wizard next time, when I create Strategic Phasing</strong> check box.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To create an Strategic Phasing diagram manually when <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Phasing Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> is disabled</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Strategic Roadmap Package and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Strategic Phasing</strong>.</li><li><p>Type the diagram name.</p></li><li>Press Enter. <br />The blank Strategic Phasing chart is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8600af1e-c713-4272-82fa-719de9571ce4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To restore the usage of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram Creation Wizard" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Strategic Phasing Creation Wizard]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<strong> Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog select the <strong>UAF</strong> options group.</li><li>In the General properties, set the <strong>Show Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart Creation Wizard</strong> property value to <em>true</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Adding Capabilities</h3><p>To add a new Capability</p><hr /><ul><li>On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability</strong>.<br />An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click<strong> Add Existing </strong>&gt;<strong> Capability</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a Capability and click the <strong>+</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done</li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Adding Sub Capabilities</h3><p>To add a new Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select <strong>Add New Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To add an existing Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click<strong> Add Existing</strong> &gt; <strong>Sub Capability</strong>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select <strong>Add Existing Sub Capability</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>From the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a Capability and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.<br /><br /></li></ol><h3>Adding Capability Provisions</h3><p>To add a new Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click <strong>Add New</strong> &gt; <strong>Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select <strong>Add <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">New</span> Capability Provision</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>Follow the steps of the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>.</strong></span></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><p>     </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3898968-3db5-4cb5-97c7-fafc68857545"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Displaying all Sub Capabilities</h3><p>To display all Sub Capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61c1ff3e-83db-4053-9349-dca0dc439067"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[highlighted]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To display deleted Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click<strong> Select All</strong> to display all removed Sub Capabilities.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Deleting Capabilities and Sub Capabilities</h3><p>To remove a Capability or Sub Capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability or Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> Remove</strong><strong> From Chart </strong>button.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete a Capability or Sub Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capabilityor Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong><strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Showing or hiding Capability Provisions area</h3><p>You can choose whether you want to show or hide selected Capability's Provisions in the Capability Provisions area in the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show or hide Capability Provisions</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart, select a Capability.</li><li>On the Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart toolbar, click the Show/Hide Capability Provisions Area button.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_hide_capability_provisions_area_strategic_phasing.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="160ff364-b1e1-48aa-bdbe-cf2b924b5706"><ac:rich-text-body>The bars in the Capability Provisions Area are displayed in colors according to the viewpoints.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Editing Capability Provision</h3><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision that you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capability Provision</h3><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision that you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol><h3>Aligning Bar Positions</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">You can adjust the visible part of the Gantt chart bars. You can either shift the bars to the left based on the selected bar's start date and marking options, or keep the default visibility, determined by the earliest bar date and marking options.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">In the image below, the bars were moved to left to align with a selected <em>Financial Planning </em>bar.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_bars_left.png" /></ac:image></span></p><p><br /></p><h3>Show/hide the sub-resources in Capability Provisions panel</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">You can show or hide the sub-resources in the Capability provision panel. The Show/Hide Sub Resources in the Capability Provision area button alows you to change the sub-resources visibility.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_hide_sub_resources_in_the_capability_provision_area.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h4 style="margin-left: 40.0px;">Adding, editing, and removing In Service and Out of Service dates</h4><p>When the sub-resources are shown, you can add, edit, or remove the In Service and Out of Service dates for the sub-resources directly in the Capability Provision area.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add the In Service / Out of Service dates</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Capability Provision area, right-click the needed sub-resource.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Add In Service / Out of Service</strong> command. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Follow the steps of the wizard.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To edit the In Service / Out of Service dates</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Capability Provision area, right-click the needed sub-resource.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Edit In Service / Out of Service</strong> command. The <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> opens.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Modify the needed dates.</span></li><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">To remove the In Service / Out of Service dates</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Capability Provision area, right-click the needed sub-resource.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, click <strong>Remove In Service / Out of Service</strong> command.</li></ol><h3>Set the sub-resources depth in Capability Provisions panel</h3><p>You can control the number of sub-resources displayed in the Capability Provisions panel. The <strong>Sub-resources Depth in Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart</strong> project option lets you define the hierarchy level of sub-resources shown in the Capability Provisions panel across the Strategic Phasing, CV-3, StV-3, NCV-3, and Cr Gantt Charts.</p><p> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="sub_resource_depth.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Legends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Filtering table data" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563982 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06800: Working with Strategic Processes diagram

- page_id: `249563982`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563982/Working+with+Strategic+Processes+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:04.457+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Processes
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies

1. Create Security Process
2. Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.
3. Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.
4. Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Security Processes diagram for Security Processes hierarchies</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Security Process</li><li>Create Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, or reuse them from the Security Taxonomy view.</li><li>Draw Is Capable To Perform relationship between Security Enclaves, Operation or Resource Mitigations, and Security Processes.</li><li>Draw Compositions (whole-part relationships) between Security Processes.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 06801: Working with Strategic States diagram

- page_id: `249563939`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563939/Working+with+Strategic+States+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:03.312+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic States
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1349294402

1349294405

1349294403

To create a Strategic States diagram

1. Create or select a Desirer (for example, Capability, Operational Performer, Capability Configuration)
2. Create or select an Actual State.
3. Create a Desires relationship between a Desirer and an Actual State.
4. Create or select an Achiever (for example, Actual Organization, Actual Resource) to achieve the Actual State.
5. Create an Achieves between an Achiever and Actual State.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7ec78ac-59b9-4f49-b1d1-e006c530b9ee"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349294402</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ef7afa0-a1e2-429b-9289-52772557d6a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349294405</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66ceaf01-71fc-45dd-bf5e-14fefa3515ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349294403</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create a Strategic States diagram</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Create or select a Desirer (for example, Capability, Operational Performer, Capability Configuration)</p></li><li><p>Create or select an Actual State.</p></li><li><p>Create a Desires relationship between a Desirer and an Actual State.</p></li><li>Create or select an Achiever (for example, Actual Organization, Actual Resource) to achieve the Actual State.</li><li>Create an Achieves between an Achiever and Actual State.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563944 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06802: Working with Strategic Structure diagram

- page_id: `249563944`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563944/Working+with+Strategic+Structure+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:28.087+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Structure
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1242277357

1242277360

1242277356

**On this page**

1242277359

##### topCreating a diagram

To create an Strategic Structure diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Structure and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select **Create Diagram**>**Strategic Structure**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click **Create Diagram**, search for **Strategic Structure**and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Whole Life Enterprise element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.

To create an Whole Life Enterprise in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Whole Life Enterprise and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.

To create an Whole Life Enterprise in a Containment tree

1. In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select **Create Element**. Search for the Whole Life Enterprise and select it. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='dragg_whole_life_enterprise.png']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Whole Life Enterprise (or other appropriate element).

For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space=''].

##### Connecting the Operational Performers

When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.

To connect the elements with the Composition relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. From the Smart Manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Directed Composition. You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Structural Parts (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.

To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship

1. Select an element, which will be the Composition End.
2. In the diagram palette, click the black arrow near the **Organization in Enterprise** and choose **Directed** **Aggregation**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.

##### Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity

To change the role name

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Edit Name . The default name will be displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.change_role_name.png']

You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To specify multiplicity

1. Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.
2. At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values. [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.change_multiplicity.png']

You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.

##### Displaying created structure

If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:

- Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.
- Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.
- Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''] .
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']** dialog, select **Association.** If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the **Resource Association** also.
4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.display_related.png']

To display a created structure on a diagram (Attribute Compartment Notation)

1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
2. Select the element ant click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic_compartments.png']
3. In the Compartments menu, select **Attributes**. When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.

To display a created structure on a diagram (Structure (Nested elements) Notation)

1. 
  1. Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.
  2. Do one of the following:
    - Select the element, click the Compartments [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space=''] , and choose Structure .
    - Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select Display > [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] .
  3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space=''] dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.
  4. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='strategic.str.display_related_nested.png']

1242283443

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulation' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9bee471-5e35-4e92-8a46-714337587da3"><ac:parameter ac:macro-id="fb1c04e8-1911-4a38-95a0-f49b8ab0a7c5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1242277359</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7f96904b-939d-4d80-9234-c113de640666"><ac:parameter ac:name="">top</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Creating a diagram</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an Strategic Structure diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Structure and do one of the following:</span><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Strategic Structure</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="202" ac:width="319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_structure_diagram.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Strategic Structure </strong>and select it.</span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</span></li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Creating an element</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using Whole Life Enterprise element, but the same is valid for other structural elements.<br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> in a diagram</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the diagram palette, click the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="237" ac:width="484"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_whole_life_enterprise.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To create an <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> in a Containment tree</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the Containment tree, right click the Strategic Structure package and from the shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Element</strong>. Search for the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> and select it.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="581" ac:width="351"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_strategic_whole_life_enterprise_containment.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li><li>Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="222" ac:width="691"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragg_whole_life_enterprise.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Whole Life Enterprise</span> (or other appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b78b18b-abe1-4fe3-a29c-3e9af9c1ac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connecting the Operational Performers</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When you have Operational Performer created, you can start connecting them using the Composition and Aggregation relationships.</span></p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To connect the elements with the Composition relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the Smart Manipulator, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> which appears after you select the element, choose</span> Directed Composition.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0780fc43-c960-4eb5-907e-da21eeb36ba3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">You can also select the Composition relationship from the diagram palette.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_composition_palette.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="168" ac:width="907"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_draw_composition.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>Once the Composition relationships is created, in the Containment tree, you can see the Composite element and note the Structural Parts (Part Properties) displayed within the namespace of the Composite element and how the element type and multiplicity is shown.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Aggregation relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an element, which will be the Composition End.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the diagram palette, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">click the black arrow near the <strong>Organization in Enterprise</strong> and choose <strong>Directed</strong> <strong>Aggregation</strong></span>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="398" ac:width="339"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_select_aggregation.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the element which is the Aggregation End and draw the relationship to the Reference End.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Changing the role name and specifying multiplicity</h3><p>To change the role name</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the relationship.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Edit Name</strong>. The default name will be displayed.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="188" ac:width="386"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.change_role_name.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can edit the default name on the diagram, in the Containment tree, or in the role's <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>. </p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To specify multiplicity</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the selected Role end of the Composite relationship.</li><li>At the bottom of the shortcut menu, choose one of the default multiplicity values.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="660" ac:width="389"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.change_multiplicity.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>You can always change the multiplicity value on the diagram or in the specification window of the role element.</p><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><h3>Displaying created structure</h3><p>If you already have created a structure in the Containment tree, you can display it on the diagram. There are three ways to display the structure:</p><ul><li>Relationship (direct) Notation. If you choose using this notation, the structure will be displayed on a diagram pane with the elements connected with relationships.</li><li>Attribute Compartment Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed as the attributes in the Attributes compartment.</li><li>Structure (Nested elements) Notation. If you choose using this notations, the structure will be displayed in the Structure compartment.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram (Direct Notation)</p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Related Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog, select <strong>Association.</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ecb5d55f-6aa4-4cd0-80a3-94313a1f4d44"><ac:rich-text-body>If Resource Artifacts (or other structural elements) were related using the Resource Association relationship, select the <strong>Resource Association</strong> also.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click<strong> OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="864" ac:width="516"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.display_related.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Attribute Compartment Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Select the element ant click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic_compartments.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Compartments menu, select <strong>Attributes</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="510760c9-d0df-4fec-8215-ca1801ea4b29"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When connecting the elements with Composition/Aggregation relationships, the roles are Association Ends. In such case, the roles will not be displayed in the Attributes compartment, only in the Structure compartment.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p>To display a created structure on a diagram <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">(Structure (Nested elements) Notation)</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>Drag the selected element with the part properties on to the diagram.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the element, click the Compartments <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and choose <strong>Structure</strong>.</li><li>Right-click that element, and from its shortcut menu, select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Display Parts/Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong>dialog, select the parts you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="713" ac:width="680"><ri:attachment ri:filename="strategic.str.display_related_nested.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p style="text-align: right;"><ac:link ac:anchor="top"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Back to top]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c9928bb-8bd2-4e45-b832-52b0b2427d9e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1242283443</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying related elements" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Smart manipulation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563962 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06803: Working with Strategic Taxonomy diagram

- page_id: `249563962`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563962/Working+with+Strategic+Taxonomy+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:28.455+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1162351586

1162351588

1162365942

**On this page**

1162351587

##### Creating a diagram

To create an Strategic Taxonomy diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Strategic Taxonomy and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Strategic Taxonomy . [ATTACHMENT filename='create_strategic_taxonomy.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Strategic Taxonomy and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements.

To create a Capability in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Capability and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_capability.png']
2. Name the element.

To create a Capability in the Containment tree

1. Right click the Strategic Taxonomy package and from the shortcut menu, select Create Element . Select the Capability . 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Name the element.
3. Drag the created element from the Containment tree to the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability.

##### Connect the elements

When you have Capability created, you can start connecting them using the Generalization relationship.

To connect the elements with the Generalization relationship

1. Select an element from which the Generalization will be drawn.
2. From the smart manipulator, which appears after you select the element, choose Generalization. You can also select the Generalization relationship from the diagram palette. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Move the mouse pointer over the target element and click to finish the action.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If there is a need, you can make the Generalization target element abstract.

To make an element abstract

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Make sure that All is chosen for Properties .
3. Search for Is Abstract and set it to true .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To view the inherited properties

1. Select an element and open its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] .
2. Click Attributes, Properties, or Relations property group.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']
