<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563972 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06804: Working with Strategic Taxonomy table

- page_id: `249563972`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563972/Working+with+Strategic+Taxonomy+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:04.074+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Taxonomy
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a Strategic Taxonomy table

- Click the Add New button to add a Capability. Enter a name and define the documentation.
- Click the Add Existing button and in the Select Capability dialog, select the desired Capabilities. Define the documentation.

You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:

- Complete tree
- Compact tree
- List

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To create a Strategic Taxonomy table</p><hr /><div class="wiki-content"><ul><li>Click the <strong>Add New</strong> button to add a Capability. Enter a name and define the documentation.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing</strong> button and in the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select the desired Capabilities. Define the documentation.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>You can change the table display mode by choosing one of the following:</p><ul><li>Complete tree</li><li>Compact tree</li><li>List</li></ul></div>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563932 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06805: Working with Strategic Typical Deployment diagram

- page_id: `249563932`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563932/Working+with+Strategic+Typical+Deployment+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:06:29.409+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Strategy > Strategic Roadmap
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

545192467

100.00002%

545192470

545192464

**On this page**

545192469

##### Creating Strategic Typical Deployment table

The context of the Strategic Typical Deployment table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Strategic Structure' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Actual Responsible Resources, and the columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Phases***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a StrategicTypical Deployment table

To create an empty Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a Strategic Roadmap package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > Strategic Typical Deployment .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > Strategic Typical Deployment .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. A***Phases***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

To remove Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Typical Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Phases***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the Strategic Typical Deployment table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627893252

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Logging background actions' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="144bb1a6-1cc2-41f2-b491-1e7c02f8b9a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">545192467</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9a20ab0-99e3-46d7-99d6-847484d33527"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">100.00002%</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="id">545192470</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="15e6475f-547e-487d-baad-cd72439dff4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">545192464</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8def6bb3-e2e7-4b62-9ad0-027247a21c65" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="777768f7-0d96-44e2-9f3f-d27df39297a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">545192469</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating Strategic Typical Deployment table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the Strategic Typical Deployment table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Strategic Structure" /></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The content of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the Strategic Typical Deployment table are Actual Responsible Resources, and the columns are Capabilities.</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="609bc2a1-2033-436c-be60-3eac966fcacb">Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the <strong><em>Phases </em></strong>relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="609bc2a1-2033-436c-be60-3eac966fcacb">Resources are related to Capabilities using the </ac:inline-comment-marker><em><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="609bc2a1-2033-436c-be60-3eac966fcacb">Exhibits</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="609bc2a1-2033-436c-be60-3eac966fcacb"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></em><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="609bc2a1-2033-436c-be60-3eac966fcacb">relationship when the table cell is filled.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.</span> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-2-11_14-17-4.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.</p><p>There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the Strategic Typical Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a Strategic Typical Deployment table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a Strategic </span>Typical Deployment table</h4><p>To create an empty Strategic Typical Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a Strategic Roadmap package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Strategic Typical Deployment</strong>.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>Strategic Typical Deployment</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><p>A<em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Phases</strong> </span></em>relationship between the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c7c1f0af-c39a-4018-b87b-1030ce583f6b">context element (Actual Enterprise Phase)</ac:inline-comment-marker> and an added Capability is added in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6cdec1b-3c94-432e-961c-856ebfd8a021"><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.</li><li>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.</li><li>versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.</li><li>Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Removing Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a Strategic Typical Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6921e89e-e24b-44c9-95df-eec84e7260a8">Exhibits</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more Strategic Typical Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the <strong><em>Phases </em></strong>relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a Strategic Typical Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Resources from a Strategic Typical Deployment table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span>The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the Strategic Typical Deployment table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4568d249-064f-48f3-b981-1c87fc12b3d3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627893252</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Logging background actions" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562881 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06806: Working with StV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `249562881`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562881/Working+with+StV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:56.281+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page**

##### Creating StV-3 diagram

When you choose to create StV-3 diagram, **StV-3 Creation Wizard** opens.

To create a StV-3 diagram from the wizard

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click StV-3 Capability Phasing .
3. In the StV-3 Capability Phasing dialog, click Add . StV-3 Creation Wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the wizard.
5. Click Finish when you are done.

If you do not wish to use the StV-3 Creation Wizard next time, clear the check box near “Show the wizard next time, when I create StV-3”.

Also you can disable the wizard. Go to **Options** > **Environment**. In the **Environment Options** dialog go to **UAF** tab. In the General properties, set **Show CV-3/ StV-3 Creation Wizard each Time** 
**Creating New Chart** to false.

To create a StV-3 diagram manually

1. From the Diagrams menu, select Create Diagram .
2. Click StV-3 Capability Phasing .
3. In the StV-3 Capability Phasing dialog click Add .
4. Specify the diagram name and create or select the owner of the diagram. You can select as a diagram owner a view or viewpoint that is already created in the UAF project template.
5. Click OK . The blank StV-3 diagram is created.

##### Adding capabilities to StV-3 chart

To add a new capability

- On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add a new capability provision

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a capability provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the Capability Provision Creation Wizard .
4. Click Finish , when you are done.

To add an existing capability

1. On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing . The Select element dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding sub capabilities to StV-3 chart

To add a new sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to create a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add new Sub Capability .
3. On the diagram pane, an empty line for the newly created capability appears.

To add an existing sub capability

1. Select the capability for which you want to add a sub capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the StV-3 diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - On the shortcut menu of the selected capability, click Add existing Sub Capability .
3. In the opened Select Capability dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.

##### Removing capabilities and sub capabilities from StV-3 chart

To remove a capability or sub capability from the chart

1. Select a capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Remove From Chart .

To delete a capability or sub capability from the model

1. Select a capability or sub capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete .

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Display all sub capabilities in StV-3 chart

To display all sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.
2. In the StV-3 chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space=''] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

<strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, select a capability (or several capabilities) and click the<strong> +</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ed3d8cc-a0d7-48ed-96a2-17b53ea0b06a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The element name in gray indicates that the element was used from another project and/or is read-only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3>Removing capabilities and sub capabilities from StV-3 chart</h3><p>To remove a capability or sub capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Remove From Chart</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete a capability or sub capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a capability or sub capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55f7247d-0bb5-4fe1-a13a-2815a70f9122"><ac:rich-text-body>The capability name becomes <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[highlighted]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To display deleted Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click<strong> Select All</strong> to display all removed Sub Capabilities.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Display all sub capabilities in StV-3 chart</h3><p>To display all sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the StV-3 chart toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol><h3>Editing Capability Provision</h3><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capability Provision</h3><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562895 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06807: Working with StV-5 table

- page_id: `249562895`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562895/Working+with+StV-5+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:56.934+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413145832

413145834

413145831

**On this page**

413145833

##### Creating StV-5 table

The context of the StV-5 table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Actual Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StV-1 diagram' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the StV-5 table is Actual Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. Rows of the StV-5 table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

In earlier versions, the Exhibits relationship was created between the Type of Actual Resources and Capability. Now, the Exhibits relationship is created directly between the Actual Resource and Capability.

Deploy the Actual Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (Deployed and No Longer Used) using the Responsible For relationship. Actual Resources are set as the Actual Resource for both Actual Project Milestones.

There can be one or more Actual Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The Deployed Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is started to use. The No Longer Used Milestone defines the time when the Actual Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

Actual Resources are related to Capabilities using the Exhibits relationship.

To work with the StV-5 table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a StV-5 table

To create an empty StV-5 table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click an StV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. AnExhibitsrelationship between the context element and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Actual Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add an Actual Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Actual Resources and specify deployment and no longer used dates for them.
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Removing Capabilities from an StV-5 table

To remove Capabilities from an StV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An Exhibitsrelationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more Strategic Actual Deployment tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the Exhibits relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in an StV-5 table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Actual Resources from an StV-5 table

Toremovethe deployment of the Actual Resources from an StV-5 table

1. Click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select an Actual Resource and click the**-** button. Actual Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the StV-5 table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

229836933

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562899 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06808: Working with StV-5 typical table

- page_id: `249562899`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562899/Working+with+StV-5+typical+table
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:57.153+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint > MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

627968607

627968609

627968605

**On this page**

627968608

##### Creating StV-5 typical table

The context of the StV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase. The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with StV-5 table' space=''] view.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The content of the StV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the StV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.

Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.

Resources are related to Capabilities using the ***Exhibits***relationship when the table cell is filled.

Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the ***Responsible For*** relationship. Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The ***Responsible For*** relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.

There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase.

To work with the StV-5 typical table

1. .
2. .
3. .
4. .

###### emptyCreate a StV-5 typical table

To create an empty StV-5 typical table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Right-click a StV-5 package as the diagram owner and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
  - Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select Create Diagram > StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical) .
2. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''] opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.

You can click Finish in the first step of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard' space=''].**Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.

###### rowsAdd Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table

1. Click the Add Rows button.
2. In the Select Actual Organizational Resources dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.
3. Click OK when you are done.

###### columnsAdd Capabilities as columns in the table

To add Capabilities as table columns

1. Click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capabilities dialog, choose Capabilities.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. An***Exhibits***relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.

###### cellsAdd Resources in table cells as content in the table

To add Resources in table cells as content in the table

1. Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.
2. Click the + button that appears at the right of the selected cell.
3. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space=''] , select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.
4. Click **Finish** when you are done. The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.

##### Removing Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table

To remove Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click the Add/ Remove Columns button.
2. In the Select Capability dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.
3. Click **OK** when you are done. Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.An ***Exhibits***relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.If you have more StV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the ***Exhibits***relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.

To hide Capabilities in a StV-5 typical table

1. In the table toolbar, click Columns .
2. In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.

##### Removing Resources from a StV-5 typical table

To remove the deployment of the Resources from a StV-5 typical table

1. Double-click the cell you want to edit.
2. Select a Resource and click the**-** button. The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model.

##### Removing rows from the table

To remove a row from the table

1. Select a row you want to remove.
2. In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table. The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.

##### Generating a report

There is a predefined report for the StV-5 typical table.

To generate the report

1. In the table toolbar, click Report .
2. Select the location and type the name for the report file.
3. Click Generate .

627973640

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Typical Resources Deployment Wizard' space='']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="02deb361-f3f3-4477-ab82-0a99f508a157"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627968607</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efd2d4a6-83bb-4661-9d27-98debc84f9c9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627968609</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67b9f18b-6b3c-4015-a7e5-da7c77b5aac4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627968605</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a7683bda-3c89-4932-82cd-3f91ea46cb87" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="149f3a68-1c64-4ea1-a0a6-4859a0752e42"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627968608</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3 class="with-breadcrumbs">Creating StV-5 typical table</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The context of the StV-5 typical table is the Actual Enterprise phase.  The Actual Enterprise Phase can have Start and End dates defined. Those dates can be reused for deploying Resources. The Enterprise Phase element is related to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with StV-5 table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StV-5]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> view.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="typical_resources_deployment.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The content of the StV-5 typical table are Resources used by Actual Responsible Resources (Actual Organizations, Actual Posts, and Actual Persons) to realize Capabilities in a particular time period of the enterprise. The rows of the StV-5 typical table are Actual Responsible Resources, and columns are Capabilities.</p><p>Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are related using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the Capability is added as a table column.</p><p>Resources are related to Capabilities using the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship when the table cell is filled.</p><p>Deploy the Resources that are necessary for the Actual Responsible Resource to accomplish the selected Capability. Actual Responsible Resources are related to Actual Project Milestones (In Service and Out Of Service) using the <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources are set as the versionReleased for In Service Milestone and versionWithdrawn for Out of Service Milestone.</span> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2021-2-11_14-17-4.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The <em><strong>Responsible For</strong></em> relationship is created in the model when the table cell is filled with Resources.</p><p>There can be one or more Resources related to the Actual Organizational Resource. The VerionReleased Milestone defines the time when the Resource is started to use. The VersionWithdrawn Milestone defines the time when the Resource is no longer used. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">We recommend using the timeframe from the Actual Enterprise Phase. </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To work with the StV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="empty"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Create a StV-5 typical table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="rows"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="columns"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Capabilities as columns in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link ac:anchor="cells"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Add Resources in table cells as content in the table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</span></li></ol><p><span class="confluence-link"><br /></span></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e729073a-7a45-4078-999a-1090c09e421d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">empty</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Create a StV-5 typical</span> table</h4><p>To create an empty StV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right-click a StV-5 package as the diagram owner and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li><li>Right-click an Actual Enterprise Phase and select <strong>Create Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong>StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping (typical)</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens. Follow the steps of the wizard.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="df48fef4-8d07-4d70-8183-b442629bd475"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can click Finish in the first step of the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Actual Resources Deployment Creation Wizard" /></ac:link>. </strong>Then an empty table is created and you will need to add rows and columns manually.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dcaf8c6b-a839-4ebc-a4b9-75d2529195f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">rows</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</span></h4><p>To add Actual Organizational Resources as rows in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add Rows </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Actual Organizational Resources</strong> dialog, choose Actual Organizational Resources.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b0cd39-cf76-49f2-a56a-f7e14c54e52e"><ac:parameter ac:name="">columns</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Capabilities as columns in the table</span></h4><p>To add Capabilities as table columns</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the<strong> Add/ Remove Columns </strong>button.</li><li>In the<strong> Select Capabilities</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb2dee6a-f048-4967-9303-5566fa427b05"><ac:rich-text-body><p>An<em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element (Actual Enterprise Phase) and an added Capability is added in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h4><span class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d323a01-8c43-4fb4-ab92-2179fc127d29"><ac:parameter ac:name="">cells</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Add Resources in table cells as content in the table</span></h4><p>To add Resources in table cells as content in the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the cell wherein you want to add a Resource.</li><li>Click the <strong>+</strong> button that appears at the right of the selected cell.</li><li>In the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></strong>, select Resources and specify In Service and Out Of Serice dates for them.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f6cdec1b-3c94-432e-961c-856ebfd8a021"><ac:rich-text-body><ol><li>The Exhibits relationship is created between Resource and Capability.</li><li>The Actual Project Milestones are created. If the milestone with the same chosen dates already exists, it is reused.</li><li>versionReleased and versionWithdrawn tags are filled.</li><li>Responsible For relationship is created between Actual Resource and milestones.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h3>Removing Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove Capabilities from a StV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click the <strong>Add/ Remove Columns</strong> button.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Capability</strong> dialog, choose Capabilities to remove them from a table.</li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01d15d9f-1a14-49da-87e1-582976e1efce"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Capabilities are removed only from the table. They are not removed from the model.</li><li>An <em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Exhibits</strong> </span></em>relationship between the context element and a removed Capability is removed from the model.</li><li>If you have more StV-5 typical tables where the same Actual Enterprise Phase and Capability are used, then removing the <em><strong>Exhibits</strong> </em>relationship, the Capability is removed from other diagrams as well.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To hide Capabilities in a StV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Columns</strong>.</li><li>In the open menu, click the Capabilities you want to hide.</li></ol><h3>Removing Resources from a StV-5 typical table</h3><p>To remove the deployment of the Resources from a StV-5 typical table</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the cell you want to edit.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a Resource and click the<strong> -</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="623de54d-af2e-4c4d-b0a5-0fec174f6335"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The Resource is no longer deployed for the particular Actual Responsible Resource, but it is not removed from the model. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Removing rows from the table</h3><p>To remove a row from the table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a row you want to remove.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the table toolbar, click Remove From Table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="33d4780d-d26c-4c4d-aef3-14d9b95efbce"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The row is only hidden from the table. The deployment is not removed.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><h3>Generating a report</h3><p>There is a predefined report for the StV-5 typical table.</p><p><br /></p><p>To generate the report</p><hr /><ol><li>In the table toolbar, click <strong>Report</strong>.</li><li>Select the location and type the name for the report file.</li><li>Click <strong>Generate</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d27b276b-c5a0-44b0-ab4d-1d9dad4cdacc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">627973640</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Typical Resources Deployment Wizard" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563873 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06809: Working with Summary and Overview diagram

- page_id: `249563873`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563873/Working+with+Summary+and+Overview+diagram
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:59:25.919+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Summary and Overview
- labels: ['unrestored-unknown-attachment', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Summary and Overview** diagram shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. It serves as a planning guide in the initial phases of architecture development. Upon completion of architecture, it summarizes findings and any conducted analysis.

##### Creating a diagram

To create a Summary and Overview diagram

1. In the Containment Tree, select the Summary and Overview view specification package and do one of the following:
  - From the selected package's shortcut menu, select Create Diagram > Summary and Overview . [ATTACHMENT filename='summary_and_overview_new.png']
  - In the modeling tool's main menu, click Create Diagram , search for Summary and Overview , and select it.
2. Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.

##### Creating an element

When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements. An example is described using the Architectural Description element, but the same is valid for other available elements.

To create an element in a diagram

1. In the diagram palette, click the Architectural Description (or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane. [ATTACHMENT filename='summary_and_overview_new_element_palette.png']
2. Name the element.

To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)

1. Copy a list in your resource.
2. In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the Paste Special dialog, choose Element .
3. From the Select Type dialog, choose Capability Configuration (or another appropriate element).

##### Connect the elements

When you have an Architectural Description(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the Architectural Description will be connected to another Architectural Description using the Architectural Reference relationship.

To connect the elements with the Architectural Reference relationship

1. From the diagram palette, choose Architectural Reference.
2. On the diagram pane, click the source Architectural Description element and do one of the following:
  - Click the target Architectural Description if it is already drawn on the diagram pane.
  - Click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a new Architectural Description element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The <strong>Summary and Overview</strong> diagram shows a quick overview of an architecture description and a summary of the analysis. It serves as a planning guide in the initial phases of architecture development. Upon completion of architecture, it summarizes findings and any conducted analysis.</span></p><h3>Creating a diagram</h3><p><br /></p><p>To create a Summary and Overview diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment Tree, select the Summary and Overview view specification package and do one of the following:<ul><li> From the selected package's shortcut menu, select <strong>Create Diagram </strong>&gt;<strong> Summary and Overview</strong>.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="350" ac:width="310"><ri:attachment ri:filename="summary_and_overview_new.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the modeling tool's main menu, click <strong>Create Diagram</strong>, search for <strong>Summary and Overview</strong>,<strong> </strong>and select it.</li></ul></li><li>Name a diagram or leave it with the default name.</li></ol><h3>Creating an element</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">When the diagram is created, you can start creating the appropriate elements</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. An example is described using the Architectural Description element, but the same is valid for other available elements.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create an element in a diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, click the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>(or another appropriate element) and then click the appropriate place on the diagram pane.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="summary_and_overview_new_element_palette.png" /></ac:image></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Name the element.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create the elements from other resources (e.g. Word, Excel, HTML)</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy a list in your resource.</li><li>In a diagram, press Ctrl+V and from the <strong>Paste Special</strong> dialog, choose <strong>Element</strong>. </li><li>From the <strong>Select Type</strong> dialog, choose <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Capability Configuration</span> (or another appropriate element).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e95dfc12-8507-4b0f-a54d-55c89892323c"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about creating the elements from other resources, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating elements from other resources" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Connect the elements</h3><p><br /></p><p>When you have an <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span>(or another appropriate element) created, you can start connecting it to other elements. In this example, the <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span> will be connected to another <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Architectural Description</span> using the Architectural Reference relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect the elements with the Architectural Reference relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>From the diagram palette, choose Architectural Reference.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">On the diagram pane, click the source Architectural Description element and do one of the following:</span></p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click the target Architectural Description if it is already drawn on the diagram pane.</span></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click anywhere on the diagram pane to create a new Architectural Description element.</span></p></li></ul></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:image ac:border="true"><ri:attachment ri:filename="summary_and_overview_connect.png" /></ac:image></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562677 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06810: Working with SvcV-5 matrix

- page_id: `249562677`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562677/Working+with+SvcV-5+matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:49.554+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Services viewpoint > SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix
- labels: ['create-svcv-5-matrix', 'build-svcv-5-matrix', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix describes the mapping betweenServices and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.

To create a SvcV-5 matrix

1. Specify the Row Scope (Services).
2. Specify the Column Scope (Operational Activities).
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh.png'] .

Services maps to Operational Activities using theSupportsrelationship.

To map a Service to an Operational Activity

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>SvcV-5 Services to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix describes the mapping <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">between </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Service</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">s and Operational Activities directly or using the implied relations.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To create a SvcV-5 matrix</p><hr /><ol><li>Specify the <strong>Row Scope</strong> (Services).</li><li>Specify the <strong>Column Scope</strong> (Operational Activities).</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Refresh" ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>Services maps to Operational Activities using the<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Supports </span>relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map a Service to an Operational Activity</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968320 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06811: Working with Symbol styles

- page_id: `243968320`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968320/Working+with+Symbol+styles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:18.689+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Formatting symbols
- labels: ['style-engine', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Warning

601916684

INLINE

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Warning

601916688

INLINE

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Warning

601916687

INLINE

#### WARNING: Warning

Warning

Before you begin working on symbol styles in a Teamwork Cloud project, you need to lock the symbols styles for edit first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

In the**Project Options** dialog, click the **Symbol Styles** option group, to expand the tree hierarchy of all the styles defined within the project. You may use as many of these styles as you wish.

**Shape** and **Path** trees have the inner structure to help you find the model element for which you want to change the symbol style. The right side of the dialog contains possible choices and the instruments to manage them.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Before changing symbol styles, we advise you to make a copy of the default style so you can come back to it if needed. You can make a copy by cloning the default style, which will create a new style. You can make changes in any selected style.

To create a new style by cloning the existing one

1. In the Symbol styles group, select Default style and click the Clone button.
2. Enter a name for the new style.
3. Change options of the new style.

As stated previously, you can create several symbol styles. You can set the desired style to be your default at any time.

To make a selected style your default style

- In the Symbol styles options group, select the style to make the default and click the Make Default button.

You can change Default symbol styles using the **Set Select Symbol Style as Default** button in the diagram toolbar. If you do not see this button, you need to change **Perspective** to the **Full feature** or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Perspectives' space=''].

You can select to apply one of the predefined styles for the existing diagrams.

To apply the selected style

- In the Symbol styles group, select the style you want to apply and click the Apply button.

You can also apply the desired options to selected diagram model elements. Click the **Apply** button in the specific element's pane.

You can move your earlier defined symbol styles between other projects or users. Simply export the defined style and pass it on for other projects or users to import.

To save the created style (export)

1. In the Symbol styles group, click the Export button. The Save dialog opens.
2. Select the location where you wish to export a style.

To import a project style

1. In the Symbol styles group, click the Import button. The Open dialog opens.
2. Select the style you wish to import (*.stl).

601916682

INLINE

**Related pages**

- Symbol Properties dialog
- Symbol styles option group
- Copy and paste symbol style
- Making your own default style for new projects

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26211323-086b-4530-b462-2d9061f1851b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">601916684</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b6d8a83-ea38-465f-a393-9d253fec3aa8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">601916688</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e2f62f23-741f-4179-8588-471e1b7b84b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">601916687</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="179a9c68-a2de-4316-947c-274ba3b2dbe5"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Before you begin working on symbol styles in a Teamwork Cloud project, you need to lock the symbols styles for edit first.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="working_with_symbol_styles_in_TWC.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Symbol Styles</strong> option group, to expand the tree hierarchy of all the styles defined within the project. You may use as many of these styles as you wish.</p><p><strong>Shape</strong> and <strong>Path</strong> trees have the inner structure to help you find the model element for which you want to change the symbol style. The right side of the dialog contains possible choices and the instruments to manage them.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="symbol_styles_in_project_options.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Before changing symbol styles, we advise you to make a copy of the default style so you can come back to it if needed. You can make a copy by cloning the default style, which will create a new style. You can make changes in any selected style.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create a new style by cloning the existing one</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> group, select <em>Default</em> style and click the <strong>Clone</strong> button.</li><li>Enter a name for the new style.</li><li>Change options of the new style.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>As stated previously, you can create several symbol styles. You can set the desired style to be your default at any time. </p><p><br /></p><p>To make a selected style your default style</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> options group, select the style to make the default and click the <strong>Make Default </strong>button.<br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6d8d4bde-4063-4003-a96e-8946935a0e33"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can change Default symbol styles using the <strong>Set Select Symbol Style as Default</strong> button in the diagram toolbar. If you do not see this button, you need to change <strong>Perspective</strong> to the <strong>Full feature</strong> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing Perspectives" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[customize perspective]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.  </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can select to apply one of the predefined styles for the existing diagrams.</p><p><br /></p><p>To apply the selected style</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> group, select the style you want to apply and click the <strong>Apply </strong>button.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="27ee4049-a79d-480d-979e-01ad7f1c2b2b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also apply the desired options to selected diagram model elements. Click the <strong>Apply</strong> button in the specific element's pane.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>You can move your earlier defined symbol styles between other projects or users. Simply export the defined style and pass it on for other projects or users to import.</p><p><br /></p><p>To save the created style (export)</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> group, click the <strong>Export</strong> button. The <strong>Save</strong> dialog opens. </li><li>Select the location where you wish to export a style.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To import a project style</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> group, click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Open</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the style you wish to import (*.stl).</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0479b0f-461f-40b4-bd5f-4b6d0766a6a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">601916682</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Symbol+Properties+dialog">Symbol Properties dialog</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Symbol+styles+option+group">Symbol styles option group</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Copy+and+paste+symbol+style">Copy and paste symbol style</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Making+your+own+default+style+for+new+projects">Making your own default style for new projects</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967269 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06812: Working with symbols of model elements

- page_id: `243967269`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967269/Working+with+symbols+of+model+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:26.951+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['resize', 'resize-shape', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Use the Sticky button

569530408

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Use the Sticky button

569530410

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Use the Sticky button

569530409

700349

To draw an element on the diagram pane

1. Select an element button on the diagram palette.
2. Click anywhere on the diagram pane.

To draw several elements of the same type on the diagram pane, do one of the following

#### EXPAND: Use the Shift key

Use the Shift key

1. Press and hold Shift.
2. Click an element button on the diagram palette.
3. Click the diagram pane. With each click, the new element of the same type is drawn.

#### EXPAND: Use the Sticky button

Use the Sticky button

1. On the diagram palette, under the Tools category, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='sticky_button.png'] Sticky button or press Z.
2. From the diagram palette, select an element button.
3. Click the diagram pane. With each click, the new element of the same type is drawn.

To draw a symbol of the existing element

1. Activate a diagram on which you wish to draw a symbol.
2. From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select an item you wish to draw.
3. Drag the selected model element onto the diagram pane.

To specify a name of the element, do one of the following

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , Name property specification cell, type the element name.
- Type the symbol name directly on the selected symbol on the diagram pane.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select an element and name it.

To draw several symbols for one element, do one of the following

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select an element and drag it to the diagram pane. Repeat it if needed.
- On the diagram pane, select a symbol, copy and then paste by pressing Ctrl+C and Ctrl+V.

To resize selected shapes to a preferred size

• Click the shape and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] at the corners of the symbol and drag to the preferred size.

To change text and compartments vertical position on shapes

- In the Symbol Properties dialog > Text Vertical Position , select one of the following:
  - Top
  - Center
  - Bottom

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To delete selected model elements, do one of the following

- From the Edit menu, select Delete .
- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='delete.png'] Delete button.
- Press Ctrl+D.
- In the Activity and Sequence diagrams, press Delete.

The model elements will be removed from the model with all their data.

To delete selected symbols, do one of the following

- From the Edit menu, select Remove from Diagram .
- On the diagram toolbar, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_from_diagram.png'] Remove from Diagram button.
- Press Delete.

569530407

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

## PAGE 06813: Working with Typical Measurements table

- page_id: `249565304`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565304/Working+with+Typical+Measurements+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T16:25:37.278+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1776767993

1776768014

1776768003

To create a Typical Measurements table

1. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Add New button and from the drop-down list select [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space=''] . The new row in the table is created. Type the name of the Property Set and specify the Measurements of the selected Property Set.
  - Click the Add Existing button to add an existing Property Set to the table.
2. Click the Evaluate Measurements button to create the Actual Measurements table automatically.

In the Typical Measurements table, you can select one of the Display Modes:

- Owned Measurements - collects only owned measurements.
- All Measurements - collects all measurements hierarchically with primitive types.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The created instances are stored in the same location as the Typical Measurements table.

1776767992

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set elements for measurement tables' space='']

The new row in the table is created.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span>Type the name of the Property Set and specify the Measurements of the selected Property Set.</li><li>Click the <strong>Add Existing </strong>button to add an existing Property Set to the table.</li></ul></li><li>Click the <strong>Evaluate Measurements</strong> button to create the Actual Measurements table automatically.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>In the Typical Measurements table, you can select one of the Display Modes:</p><ul><li><strong>Owned Measurements</strong> - collects only owned measurements.</li><li><strong>All Measurements</strong> - collects all measurements hierarchically with primitive types.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resources_display_mode.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="591b00b2-9507-4120-a97e-f2b7110f5b5a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The created instances are stored in the same location as the Typical Measurements table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7dcf5c7f-ff25-4ec9-bb53-124d65c3eb80"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1776767992</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property Set elements for measurement tables" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 06814: Working with User Interface Modeling diagrams

- page_id: `243966640`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966640/Working+with+User+Interface+Modeling+diagrams

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

86435654565

INLINE

2142654765

INLINE

1232455425

INLINE

To work with a User Interface Modeling diagram

Do one of the following:

- On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button and select User Interface Modeling Diagram . Press Enter.
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , click either the Package or the Model. From their shortcut menu select Create Diagram > Other Diagrams > User Interface Modeling Diagram .

After loading a project and creating a diagram, you can add UI modeling elements to the diagram pane.

To add new elements to the User Interface Modeling diagram pane

- Simply drag and drop the elements from the Text, Containers, Button, or Other toolbar.

To load a sample with an already created User Interface model

1. Open either the UI Modeling Samples.mdzip or UI Modeling in System Development.mdzip projects, found in <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\diagrams\User Interface Modeling .
2. After loading the sample, select the diagrams from the Index or Overview d iagrams by clicking the hyperlinks.

605235642
