<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971005 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06815: Working with Web Publisher 2.0

- page_id: `243971005`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971005/Working+with+Web+Publisher+2.0
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:19.193+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard > Web Publisher 2.0 report
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1336380615

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1336380618

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1336380618

The Web Publisher 2.0 report template comes with predefined variables and their values. Before generating a report based on the Web Publisher 2.0 template, you can add more variables to it, edit the existing ones, or delete those you do not want to include in your report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Report variables of Web Publisher 2.0.

The following table shows the variable names and values of the Web Publisher 2.0 template.

| Variable | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Author | The report's creator. |
| Title | The report's title. |
| HomeImage | Possible values:Leave it empty or unspecified to use the MagicDraw icon as the homepage image.Enter a diagram name (plain text) to display the selected diagram as the homepage image, for example, Software Development Process.Enter “mdel://” followed by a model element ID to display the element as the homepage image, for example, “mdel://_10_0EAPbeta2_8740266_1126593738250_35764_172”.Enter the absolute path of an image file on your computer to display the image as the homepage image, for example, file://d://picture//image.jpg.Enter the relative path of an image file on your computer to display the image as the homepage image. The relative path starts at the location of the Web Publisher 2.0 template: %APPDATA%\Local\.magicdraw\2021x\data\reports\Web Publisher 2.0\web (%APPDATA% is the location where the user's data are stored, for example, /images/homeimage.jpg).Enter the location of an image on the Web to display the image as the homepage image, for example, http://www.photobucket.com/image.jpg. |
| Diagram as the homepage image. |  |
| UseContextMenu | True: Shows a context menu when right-clicking a diagram element.False: Shows an element specification or opens an element, a diagram, or a page specified in any existing active hyperlink when right-clicking a diagram element. |
| SelectHistoryNode | True: Selects a node when you move backwards or forwards.False: Types "false" if you want it another way. |
| ExportLinkedFile | True: Copies a linked file from a model hyperlink into an output report folder.False: Keeps the link to an absolute path only. |
| ShowAppearsInPage | True: Shows data in a report, and opens the Appears in tab when clicking an element in the Containment tree or a diagram pane. False: Hides the Appears in tab in a generated report. |
| ShowAppliedStereotypes | True: Shows applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree.False: Does not show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree. |
| AutoNavigateHyperlink | True: Automatically navigates the active hyperlink of each element. For example, if A has an active hyperlink to B and B has an active hyperlink to C, then C will be shown when you click A.False: The behavior of navigation to active hyperlink is the same as that of MagicDraw. For example, if A has an active hyperlink to B and B has an active hyperlink to C, then B will be shown when you click A. |
| DisplayTreeElementId | True: Shows the property ID of an element in the Containment tree.False: Types "false" if you want it another way. |
| SVGDiagramResizable | True: Resizes SVG images when the browser is minimized.False: Enables SVG image linkable capability, but the resizable capability would be disabled.If the "SVGDiagramResizable" is set to True, the SVG image would be resizable on web browser, but it would not be linkable. |
| SkipElementContext | True: Skips the iteration of all elements to create velocity context classified by customized stereotypes, e.g., $CustomizedStereotypeName.False: Does not skip the iteration of all elements to create velocity context classified by customized stereotypes. |
| GotoLinkByIcon | True: Both the icon and the label of the element in the Containment tree navigate to an active hyperlink.False: The icon element navigates to the element's Specification tab even if there is an active hyperlink. |
| ExportedImageSize | The percentage (from 10 to 1000) of an exported image size. |

WorkingwithWP

The following section contains the instructions on how to work with your Web Publisher report.

To generate a Web Publisher report

1. Open the Magic Library.mdzip sample project from the <modeling_tool_home>/samples/case studies directory.
2. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**. The**Report Wizard** dialog will open. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the Select Template pane, select **Default Template** > **Web Publisher 2.0**, and click**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**.
4. You can either:
  - select the built-in report data or click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new one. Type the new report name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. In the Select Report Data pane, you can create a new set of Report Data for the Web Publisher template. The Report Data is a container for a set of custom-defined fields in the template. It can be used to group different report versions.To edit or create a variable in the report data by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''], edit or delete information for predefined custom fields or create a new one, and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].[IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] .
6. Select the element(s) from the model that you want to include in the report data, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Add_button.png'] . Select the scope of the report in the open package tree. Select the Model package if you want to have a web-based report of your entire project. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Next_button.png'] .
8. Click the[IMAGE alt='' src=''] button to locate the report file location.
9. Select the file location, type the report name, and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. A generated web report will include a number of folders and files.
10. Select the report image format: **.png*, **.jpg* or**.svg*.
11. Select an option to display empty value information, either **Empty text** or **Custom text**. In some cases, the query may return an empty value that creates blank fields in the report. The **Display empty value as** option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.
12. Select the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** check box to open the report document with the default editor.
13. After all options have been selected, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

To hide or show the Containment menu

- Click the CONTAINMENT menu to hide it, and click it again to show it.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Hiding the Containment Menu.

To expand or collapse the Containment tree

- Click the “ + ” button in the Containment tree to expand it, or click the “ - ” button to collapse it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The button to expand or collapse the Containment tree.

To reduce or increase the Containment tree width

- Drag the separator line between the containment and the content panel to reduce or increase the width of the Containment tree. The**Web Publisher 2.0**report interface uses ahorizontalscrollbar, which appears when the length of an element's name exceeds the current width of the Containment tree.The default width of the containment tree is**20%**of the**web browser**width.

To show or hide element contents

- Click and re-click the arrow button to show and hide the contents. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Showing and hiding element contents.

To show an element specification, active hyperlink, Submachine of state, or behavior of the Call Behavior action

- Click a diagram's element to show the shorcut menu for opening its specification, Active Hyperlink, Hyperlink, submachine of state, or behavior of the call behavior action. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Web Publisher 2.0 context menu.

To add an Active Hyperlink to a model

You can [add an active hyperlink to any symbol on a diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Defining+hyperlinks). There are three kinds of hyperlinks: Element, File, and URL. However, the Element type hyperlink is available for the Active Hyperlink column on the [Generic Table diagram](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Working+with+Web+Publisher+2.0#WorkingwithWebPublisher2.0-generictable) image only.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

The Web Publisher 2.0 report does not display the Generic Table diagram containing 10,000 elements or more due to the excessive size of the diagram image, making it unable to be rendered and empty.

You can navigate to the target if you double-click the hyperlink**on the diagram in the Web Publisher report. The following figure below shows the Submachine State hyperlink in the Web Publisher report.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Submachine State hyperlink in the Web Publisher report.

If you double-click the Submachine State in the above example, it navigates to the target and opens it for you as shown in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The hyperlink navigates to the target in the Web Publisher report.

Elements in the **Active Hyperlink** column in the **Generic Table** diagram link to the associate elements. If you click an element in this column, you will be navigated to the Specification page of the associated element.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

Only element type is available. File and URL address cannot navigate to the associated artifacts.

generictable

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Elements in the Active Hyperlink column in the Generic Table diagram links to the associated elements in the Specification page.

To display a property ID in the Containment tree

1. Click Tools > Report Wizard > Variable to open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Select the DisplayTreeElementId variable and enter the value true .
3. Click**OK**to show the requirement property IDs in the **Containment** tree. [IMAGE alt='' src='']The requirement property IDs also appear in the **Containment** tree of Web Publisher. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

To go to the index page

- Click the Web Publisher 2.0 at the top-left corner of the screen to go to the index page.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Shortcut to the index page.

To see the description of an element

- On the Specification tab, move your mouse over an element to see the description in a tooltip. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Showing an element description.

To show property visibility of your report

- In the **Specification** tab, select **Standard**, **Expert**, or **All** from the **Mode** drop-down menu. The mode appears in the property visibility depends upon the mode that you have selected in the modeling tool. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Property visibility mode types.

To open an Activity, a State Machine, a Collaboration, or an Interaction sub-diagram

- Click an Activity, State Machine, Collaboration, or Interaction diagram to open a sub-diagram associated with an element. [ATTACHMENT filename='Clicking a Diagram Name to Open Its Subdiagrams.png']

###### Clicking a diagram name to open its subdiagrams.

To open the sub-diagrams of a state with Submachine

1. Double-click either a state with a Submachine or a call behavior action of which behavior is specified. For example, double-clicking the last Submachine State *Request Access : Connection* in the following figure.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
When you double-click it, the associated diagram opens (see the following figure). [IMAGE alt='' src=''] State machine diagram viewed in web publisher interface.
2. Click the “**+**” button in the Containment tree to expand it, or click the “**-**” button to collapse it. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The button to expand or collapse the Containment tree.

1336380612

**Related page**

- 

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6000fe87-d1ec-4f1c-828e-67341c7d7c45"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336380615</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8e89e75e-8d5d-4e54-a389-569a32e1ea4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336380618</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e5913cc0-f66e-43c9-919b-2194b1aadedb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336380618</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Web Publisher 2.0 report template comes with predefined variables and their values. Before generating a report based on the Web Publisher 2.0 template, you can add more variables to it, edit the existing ones, or delete those you do not want to include in your report. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Variable Dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Report variables of Web Publisher 2.0.</h6><p>The following table shows the variable names and values of the Web Publisher 2.0 template.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Variable</th><th>Value</th></tr><tr><td>Author</td><td>The report's creator.</td></tr><tr><td>Title</td><td>The report's title.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>HomeImage</p></div></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Possible values:</p><ul><li>Leave it empty or unspecified to use the MagicDraw icon as the homepage image.</li><li>Enter a diagram name (plain text) to display <span>the selected diagram as the homepage </span><span>image, for example, Software Development Process.</span></li><li><span><span>Enter “mdel://” followed by a model element </span><span>ID to display the element as the homepage image, f</span><span>or example, </span><span>“</span><em><a>mdel://_10_0EAPbeta2_8740266_1126593738250_35764_172</a></em><span>”.</span></span></li><li>Enter the absolute path of an image file on your computer to display the image as the homepage image, for example, <em>file://d://picture//image.jpg</em>.</li><li>Enter the relative path of an image file on your computer to display the image as the homepage image. The relative path starts at the location of the Web Publisher 2.0 template: <em>%APPDATA%\Local\.magicdraw\2021x\data\reports\Web Publisher 2.0\web</em> (<em>%APPDATA%</em> is the location where the user's data are stored, for example, <em>/images/homeimage.jpg</em>).</li><li><p><span><span>Enter the location of an image on the Web to </span><span>display the image as the homepage image, f</span><span>or example, </span><em><a href="http://www.photobucket.com/image.jpg">http://www.photobucket.com/image.jpg</a></em><span>.</span></span></p></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Diagram as the Homepage Image.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Diagram as the homepage </span>image.</h6></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UseContextMenu</td><td colspan="1"><p>True: Shows a context menu when right-clicking a diagram element.</p><p>False: Shows an element specification or opens an element, a diagram, or a page specified in any existing active hyperlink when right-clicking a diagram element.</p></td></tr><tr><td>SelectHistoryNode</td><td><p>True: Selects a node when you move backwards or forwards.</p><p>False: T<span>ypes &quot;false&quot; if you want it another way.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>ExportLinkedFile</td><td><p>True: C<span>opies a linked file from </span><span>a model hyperlink into </span><span>an output report folder.</span></p><p>False: K<span>eeps the link to an absolute path only.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>ShowAppearsInPage</td><td><p>True: Shows <span>data in a report, and </span><span>opens the <strong>Appears in</strong> tab </span><span>when clicking an element </span><span>in the Containment tree </span><span>or a diagram pane. </span></p><p>False:<span> Hides the Appears in tab </span><span>in a generated report.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">ShowAppliedStereotypes</td><td colspan="1"><p>True: Shows applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree.</p><p>False: Does not show applied stereotypes of elements in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td>AutoNavigateHyperlink</td><td><p>True: <span>Automatically navigates the active </span><span>hyperlink of each element. For </span><span>example, if A has an active </span><span>hyperlink to B and B has an </span><span>active hyperlink to C, then C will </span><span>be shown when you click A.</span></p><p>False: <span>The behavior of navigation to </span><span>active hyperlink is the same as </span><span>that of MagicDraw. For example, </span><span>if A has an active hyperlink to B </span><span>and B has an active hyperlink to </span><span>C, then B will be shown when </span><span>you click A. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">DisplayTreeElementId</td><td colspan="1"><p>True: Shows the property ID of an element in the Containment tree.</p><p>False: <span>Types &quot;false&quot; if you want it another way.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td>SVGDiagramResizable</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>True: Resizes SVG images when the browser is minimized.</p><p>False: E<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">nables SVG image </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">linkable capability, </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">but the resizable capability </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">would be disabled.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c77e6fff-8847-4652-8357-e50bc421b15e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the &quot;SVGDiagramResizable&quot; is set to True, the SVG image would be resizable on web browser, but it would not be linkable.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">SkipElementContext</td><td colspan="1"><p>True: Skips the iteration of all elements to create velocity context classified by customized stereotypes, e.g., $CustomizedStereotypeName.</p><p>False: Does not skip the iteration of all elements to create velocity context classified by customized stereotypes.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">GotoLinkByIcon</td><td colspan="1"><p>True: Both the icon and the label of the element in the Containment tree navigate to an active hyperlink.</p><p>False: The icon element navigates to the element's <strong>Specification</strong> tab even if there is an active hyperlink.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">ExportedImageSize</td><td colspan="1">The percentage (from 10 to 1000) of an exported image size.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7802b830-21e3-48e0-ab40-c7fbae43d107"><ac:parameter ac:name="">WorkingwithWP</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The following section contains the instructions on how to work with your Web Publisher report.</p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To generate a Web Publisher report</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <em>Magic Library.mdzip</em> sample project from the <em>&lt;modeling_tool_home&gt;/samples/case studies</em> directory.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. The<strong> Report Wizard</strong> dialog will open.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Selecting Web Publisher 2.0 Template" ac:alt="Selecting Web Publisher 2.0 Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="web-publisher-2.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>In the Select Template pane, select <strong>Default Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Web Publisher 2.0</strong>, and click<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image></strong>. </p></li><li><p>You can either:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"> select the built-in report data or click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image> to create a new one. Type the new report name and description, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36a9356d-73fb-4095-b454-b92b4a1fc7a3"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>In the Select Report Data pane, you can create a new set of Report Data for the Web Publisher template. The Report Data is a container for a set of custom-defined fields in the template. It can be used to group different report versions.</li><li><p>To edit or create a variable in the report data by clicking <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Variable_button.png" /></ac:image>, edit or delete information for predefined custom fields or create a new one, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportVar_WP2.0.png" /></ac:image></h6></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li><span>Select the element(s) from the model that you want to include in the report data, and click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Add_button.png" /></ac:image><span>. Select the scope of the report in the open package tree. Select the </span><strong>Model</strong><span> package if you want to have a web-based report of your entire project.</span><br /><br /><span><ac:image ac:title="Selecting the Scope of the Web Publisher 2.0 Report" ac:alt="Selecting the Scope of the Web Publisher 2.0 Report"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select-element-scope.png" /></ac:image></span><br /><span><br /></span></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li><p>Click the<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="button-1.png" /></ac:image> button to locate the report file location.</p></li><li><p>Select the file location, type the report name, and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png" /></ac:image>. A generated web report will include a number of folders and files.</p></li><li><p>Select the report image format: <em>*.png</em>, <em>*.jpg</em> or<em> *.svg</em>.</p></li><li><p>Select an option to display empty value information, either <strong>Empty text</strong> or <strong>Custom text</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d58078fd-e4a2-4788-9066-9bbf0e6e3ba1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>In some cases, the query may return an empty value that creates blank fields in the report. The <strong>Display empty value as</strong> option is useful when you have a standard representation for blank fields.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br File and URL address cannot navigate to the associated artifacts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="26e1ca60-83fb-4dc6-a397-f3765fb00b28"><ac:parameter ac:name="">generictable</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="activehyperlink.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Elements in the Active Hyperlink column in the Generic Table diagram links to the associated elements in the Specification page.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target">To display a property ID in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong> &gt; <strong>Variable</strong> to open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li>Select the <strong>DisplayTreeElementId</strong> variable and enter the value <strong>true</strong>.</li><li><p><span>Click </span><strong>OK </strong>to show the requirement property IDs in the <strong>Containment</strong> tree.</p><h6><span><ac:image ac:title="The Requirement Property IDs in the Containment Tree in MagicDraw" ac:alt="The Requirement Property IDs in the Containment Tree in MagicDraw"><ri:attachment ri:filename="element_ID.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></span>The requirement property IDs also appear in the <strong>Containment</strong> tree of Web Publisher.</h6><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Displaying a Property ID in the Containment Tree.PNG" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target">To go to the index page</p><hr /><ul><li>Click the Web Publisher 2.0 at the top-left corner of the screen to go to the index page.</li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="GoToIndex_WP.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Shortcut to the index page.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target">To see the description of an element</p><hr /><ul><li>On the <strong>Specification </strong>tab, move your mouse over an element to see the description in a tooltip.<br /><p><ac:image ac:title="Showing Element Description" ac:alt="Showing Element Description"><ri:attachment ri:filename="webPublisher_15.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);">Showing an element description.</span></h6></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target">To show property visibility of your report</p><hr /><ul><li><p>In the <strong>Specification</strong> tab, select <strong>Standard</strong>, <strong>Expert</strong>, or <strong>All</strong> from the <strong>Mode</strong> drop-down menu. The mode appears in the property visibility depends upon the mode that you have selected in the modeling tool.<br /><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Property Visibility Mode Types.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Property visibility mode types.</h6><p>To open an Activity, a State Machine, a Collaboration, or an Interaction sub-diagram</p><hr /><ul><li><p>Click an Activity, State Machine, Collaboration, or Interaction diagram to open a sub-diagram associated with an element.<br /><br /></p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Clicking a Diagram Name to Open Its Subdiagrams.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Clicking a diagram name to open its subdiagrams.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target">To open the sub-diagrams of a state with Submachine</p><hr /><ol><li><p><span>Double-click either a state with a Submachine or a call behavior action of which behavior is specified. For example, double-clicking the last Submachine State <em>Request Access : Connection</em> in the following figure.<br /></span><span><span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Opening the Subdiagrams of a State with Submachine.png" /></ac:image><br />When you double-click it, the associated diagram opens (see the following figure).<br /></span></span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="State Machine Diagram Viewed in Web Publisher Interface.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>State machine diagram viewed in web publisher interface.</h6></li><li><p>Click the “<strong>+</strong>” button in the Containment tree to expand it, or click the “<strong>-</strong>” button to collapse it.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Button to Expand or Collapse the Containment Tree.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The button to expand or collapse the Containment tree.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5f8c8ef-0ed3-45d9-9d51-0250d630f5e8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1336380612</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related page</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="WorkingwithWP"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Working with your Web Publisher report]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561675 space=MT version=5 -->
## PAGE 06816: Wrapping MATLAB functions

- page_id: `249561675`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561675/Wrapping+MATLAB+functions
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-09-08T18:00:54.553+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

MATLAB source code contains a functions declaration. The m-file is a text file containing a list of commands written in MATLAB or Octave syntax. You can define functions and scripts in the m-file.

You can move the mathematical expressions from MATLAB source code into the model. Use the m-file to transfer functions to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'], or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Call Behavior Action' space='MED'].

Creation of elements from MATLAB source code is supported in the following diagrams:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Activity diagram' space='']

##### Wrapping the mathematical expressions from MATLAB source code

To wrap the mathematical expressions from MATLAB source code into the model, use one of the following methods:

- Drag the m-file directly to the diagram pane to create one of the following:
  - Constraint Block with its properties and parameters in the SysML Block Definition or UML Class diagrams.
  - Constraint Property and Constraint Block in the SysML Internal Block, SysML Parametric, or UML Composite Structure diagrams. Constraint Block is automatically set as the type for the newly created Constraint Property. The properties and parameters of the Constraint Block are displayed on the Constraint Property shape.
  - Call Behavior Action with its pins and opaque in the SysML or UML Activity diagrams.
- Drag the m-file onto the already existing element shape (empty or full) to set one of the following:
  - Constraints and parameters for Constraint Block in the SysML Block Definition or UML Class diagrams.
  - Type for Constraint Property by creating a new Constraint Block in SysML Internal Block, SysML Parametric, or UML Composite Structure diagrams. The properties and parameters of the newly created Constraint Block are displayed on the Constraint Property shape.
- Use the shortcut menu on the already existing element shape (empty or full). Choose Tools > Create Expression from M-File .

- The name of the created element is the same as the name of the function in the m-file. If the name of that element already exists, it will appear with an incremental number at the end of the name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- If the m-file contains multiple functions declarations, only the first function is wrapped into the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Setting the constraints and parameters for Constraint Block using m-file

To set constraints and parameters for a Constraint Block using m-file

1. In the Containment tree or on the diagram pane, select a Constraint Block or Constraint Property (with set type).
2. From the shortcut menu, choose Tools > Create Expression from M-File . The Open file dialog opens.
3. Select the m-file and click Open . The constraints and parameters are set for Constraint Block.

#### TIP: Example

Example

The following figure illustrates the function declaration in m-file. It is for least-square fitting to find the slope (m) and the y-intercept (b) of a straight-line equation from two given input parameters x and y. The constraint expression is: [m, b] = linefit(x, y). The constraint parameters are x, y, m, and b. After the function transfer into the element, its type, property, and parameter are set automatically and are displayed on the element shape.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

It is for least-square fitting to find the slope (m) and the y-intercept (b) of a straight-line equation from two given input parameters x and y. The constraint expression is: [m, b] = linefit(x, y). The constraint parameters are x, y, m, and b. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969098 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06817: Writing scripts

- page_id: `243969098`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969098/Writing+scripts
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:18.190+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations > Creating script operations
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following instructions applies to BeanShell, Groovy, JavaScript, and Jython scripts only.

##### How to access the arguments and other values from the script body?

To access an argument from the script body, you should refer to the corresponding parameter name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A script body can access the following values:

- Arguments passed to this script as parameters, such as the Context parameter in the preceding figure.
- Values passed to the structured expression, inside of which the script operation is defined. If you have an opaque behavior with a couple of parameters, you can refer to them from a script operation, which is inside of the structured expression - the body of this opaque behavior.[IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you have a structured expression specifying a derived property, validation rule, relation criterion, etc., use *THIS* to refer to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''].
- Arguments passed to a nested operation as parameters, if the script operation is defined inside of that nested operation. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Globally defined values:
  - project (current project)
  - application

##### How to simplify scripts?

To simplify the script, move complex sub calculations out of the script. Use other operations of the structured expression to specify these sub calculations and then pass the results to the script through parameters.

Let’s say we need a script operation, which counts the number of nested packages. You can use MagicDraw Open API to navigate through the model and find all the nested packages, but it would be complicated.

To simplify the script:

1. Use the Find operation to list these nested packages.
2. Use the script operation for nothing else than counting the number of the list items. Pass the result of the Find operation to this script through the PackageList parameter as the following figure shows.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

the body of this opaque behavior.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="structured_expression_opaque_behavior.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d578bcf-fcd6-42b9-9889-ff6d689fc73a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have a structured expression specifying a derived property, validation rule, relation criterion, etc., use <em>THIS</em> to refer to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Arguments passed to a nested operation as parameters, if the script operation is defined inside of that nested operation.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="structured_expression_nested_operation.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p>Globally defined values:</p><ul><li><p>project (current project)</p></li><li><p>application</p></li></ul></li></ul><h3>How to simplify scripts?</h3><p>To simplify the script, move complex sub calculations out of the script. Use other operations of the structured expression to specify these sub calculations and then pass the results to the script through parameters.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b61fd5ee-830e-4810-8ec7-6e1a87ca0937"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Let’s say we need a script operation, which counts the number of nested packages. You can use MagicDraw Open API to navigate through the model and find all the nested packages, but it would be complicated.</p><p>To simplify the script:</p><ol><li>Use the Find operation to list these nested packages.</li><li>Use the script operation for nothing else than counting the number of the list items. Pass the result of the Find operation to this script through the PackageList parameter as the following figure shows.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="count_nested_packages_script.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

## PAGE 06818: XLSX template in the Chart tool

- page_id: `243971302`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971302/XLSX+template+in+the+Chart+tool
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:10.700+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Chart tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1344514385

1344514373

INLINE

**On this page**

5

1344514375

INLINE

##### Creating chart objects and setting chart types and styles

To create a chart in accordance with the specified chart type and style, use the following code:

```text

```

Where the parameters are:

- chartType : case-sensitive type of the created chart with one of the following options:

- 
  - "Line"
  - "Column"
  - "Pie"
  - "Bar"
  - "Area"
  - "Surface"
  - "Radar"

- **chartStyle**: case-sensitive style of the created chart with one of the following options:

- 
  - Line
    - "Line" (the default style)
    - "Staked Line"
    - "100% Stacked Line"
    - "Line with Markers"
    - "Stacked Line with Markers"
    - "100% Stacked Line with Markers"
    - "3-D Line"
  - Column
    - "Clustered Column" (the default style)
    - "Stacked Column"
    - "100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Clustered Column"
    - "3-D Stacked Column"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Column"
    - "3-D Column"
  - Pie
    - "Pie" (the default style)
    - "3-D Pie"
    - "Doughnut"

- 
  - Bar
    - "Clustered Bar" (the default style)
    - "Stacked Bar"
    - "100% Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D Clustered Bar"
    - "3-D Stacked Bar"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Bar"
  - Area
    - "Area" (the default style)
    - "Stacked Area"
    - "100% Stacked Area"
    - "3-D Area"
    - "3-D Stacked Area"
    - "3-D 100% Stacked Area"
  - Surface
    - "3-D Surface" (the default style)
    - "Wireframe 3-D Surface"
    - "Contour"
    - "Wireframe Contour"
  - Radar
    - "Radar" (the default style)
    - "Radar with Markers"
    - "Filled Radar"

If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used.

- startCell : the first cell of the data table used for the chart.
- columnSize and rowSize : numeric values that determine the size of the data table.

An example of the code for a 3x5 size data table starting at cell A4:

```text

```

##### **Setting chart titles**

To set a chart title, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.

##### **Printing a chart to a report**

To print a chart to a report, use the following code:

```text

```

For example:

```text

```

A full sample of the Chart tool code is shown below:

```text

```

class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Clustered Bar&quot; (the default style)</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Stacked Bar&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;100% Stacked Bar&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D Clustered Bar&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D Stacked Bar&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D 100% Stacked Bar&quot;</p></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Area</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Area&quot; (the default style)</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Stacked Area&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;100% Stacked Area&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D class="auto-cursor-target">Surface</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;3-D Surface&quot; (the default style)</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Wireframe 3-D Surface&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Contour&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Wireframe Contour&quot;</p></li></ul></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Radar</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Radar&quot; (the default style)</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Radar with Markers&quot;</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">&quot;Filled Radar&quot;</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f5fc0ce-5a81-4783-939c-55122dcf89bf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not specify the chart style, the default style of the chosen chart type is used.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>startCell</strong>: the first cell of the data table used for the chart.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>columnSize</strong> and <strong>rowSize</strong>: numeric values that determine the size of the data table.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>An example of the code for a 3x5 size data table starting at cell A4:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60f7f17c-c2d8-4eb0-a0dc-dd33cdb00f48"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($linechart = $chart.create("Column", "3-D Column", "A4", 3, 5))]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>Setting chart titles</strong></h3><p>To set a chart title, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0bd35bb4-5494-46e2-aecc-4b278561b8e4"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[setTitle(title String)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe782819-1b41-459f-b37e-c81e7baaefb8"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.setTitle("Line Chart")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48e15ca6-fc55-4a73-8154-b975db01cd4e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you do not enter the chart title, the default title “Sample” is used.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3 class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Printing a chart to a report</strong></h3><p>To print a chart to a report, use the following code:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c617738a-2faa-4616-ac33-0eb61bb45ace"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d8b8727-c5f2-4acd-9478-4eeebce7e76c"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>A full sample of the Chart tool code is shown below:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca5284a3-c4e4-4cef-9b3d-52af82f5e42d"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#import('chart', 'com.nomagic.reportwizard.tools.ChartTool')
#set($linechart = $chart.create("Column", "3-D Column", "A4", 3, 5))
$linechart.setTitle("Sample")
$linechart.buildChart()]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span class="image-wrap"><ac:image><ri:url ri:value="https://jira.nomagic.com/secure/attachment/217998/217998_samplechart.png" /></ac:image></span><br />A chart printed to a report after applying the full sample code using the Chart tool.</h6><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567600 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06820: XML schema namespaces

- page_id: `249567600`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567600/XML+schema+namespaces
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:04.000+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Maps to a UML Package with the stereotype *XSDnamespace*. To define the “xmlns” attribute in the schema file, you must add Permission between the XSDnamespace package and XSDschema class into the model.

- The Permission name maps to the namespace shortcut.

#### PANEL: XML representation of the skeleton of a schema

XML representation of the skeleton of a schema

```text
<xs:schema
```

```text
xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema" 	targetNamespace="http://www.example.com/example">	…
```

```text
</xs:schema>
```

To generate these namespaces

- The UML model must have a Package with the name *http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema*
- The UML model must have a Package with the name *http://www.example.com/example*
- Permission with the name “xs” must be added into the model between the XMLSchema Class and the Package *http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema*
- Permission without a name must be added into the model between the XMLSchema Class and the Package *http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema*

For an XML schema namespaces UML Model example, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Schema' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Maps to a UML Package with the stereotype <em>XSDnamespace</em>. To define the “xmlns” attribute in the schema file, you must add Permission between the XSDnamespace package and XSDschema class into the model.</p><ul><li style="text-align: left;">The Permission name maps to the namespace shortcut.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="25a3f67b-f948-4360-855f-a52180b34a0c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">XML representation of the skeleton of a schema</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;xs:schema</pre><pre>	xmlns:xs=&quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</a>&quot; xmlns=&quot;<a href="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema">http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</a>&quot; <br />	targetNamespace=&quot;<a href="http://www.example.com/example">http://www.example.com/example</a>&quot;&gt;<br />	…</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:schema&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To generate these namespaces</p><hr /><ul><li>The UML model must have a Package with the name <a href="https://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"><em>http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</em></a></li><li>The UML model must have a Package with the name <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/www.example.com/example"><em>http://www.example.com/example</em></a></li><li>Permission with the name “xs” must be added into the model between the XMLSchema Class and the Package <a href="https://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"><em>http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</em></a></li><li>Permission without a name must be added into the model between the XMLSchema Class and the Package<a href="https://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"><em> http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema</em></a></li></ul><p>For an XML schema namespaces UML Model example, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Schema" /></ac:link>.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567419 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06821: XML schema to UML transformation

- page_id: `249567419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567419/XML+schema+to+UML+transformation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:33:02.034+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > Transformations
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

34120459

34120461

34120460

The XML Schema to UML transformation helps extract the abstract UML model from the XML schema model.

##### **T****yp****e mapping**

Type maps store mapping between primitive UML data types and primitive XML Schema data types. The same applies for UML to XML Schema Transformation in reverse as well. For XML Schema to UML element type mapping diagram, see [Type mapping](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/UML+to+XML+schema+transformation#UMLtoXMLschematransformation-Typemapping).

##### **Transformation results**

Once the XML schema has been transformed to UML models, you will see the following:

- The XML Schema diagrams are transformed to the Class diagrams. Unnecessary stereotypes (XSDxxxx) are discarded from the classes.
- Attributes of the classes are gathered if they were spread into several different classes.
- Attributes of the classes may be realized as associations. In this case the main class gathers all the associations of the members.
- The same principle is applied when elements are in a group shared by two or more classes. Elements (attributes) are copied into both destination classes.
- The attributes with the «XSDgroupRef» stereotype are treated as if the group relationship has been drawn and transformed accordingly. They are discarded in the UML model, and the group content (elements / attributes) placed in their place.
- Simple XML schema types (classes with the «XSDsimpleType» stereotype), which after copying and type remap are derived from any data type (UML DataType), or not derived from anything and are transformed into the UML data types.
- Simple XML schema types derived by restriction from a string, restricted by enumerating string values, and are converted into enumerations in the UML diagrams.
- The classes with the «XSDschema» stereotype are not copied into a destination model.
- The «XDSkey», «XSDkeyref», and «XSDunique» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model.
- The «XDSany», «XSDanyAttribute» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model. The «XDSnotation» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model.
- The «XDSlength», «XDSminLength», «XDSmaxLength», «XSDpattern», «XSDfractionDigits», «XSDtotalDigits», «XDSmaxExclusive», «XDSmaxInclusive», «XDSminExclusive», and «XDSminInclusive» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model.
- The XML schemas (classes with the «XSDschema» stereotype) should not be transformed, but they may contain inner classes (anonymous types of schema elements). These inner classes are transformed using the usual rules for UML type transformation, as if they were not inner classes but normal XML schema types.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The same applies for UML to XML Schema Transformation in reverse as well. For XML Schema to UML element type mapping diagram, see <span class="toc-item-body"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/UML+to+XML+schema+transformation#UMLtoXMLschematransformation-Typemapping" class="toc-link">Type mapping</a></span>.</p><h3><strong>Transformation results</strong></h3><p>Once the XML schema has been transformed to UML models, you will see the following:</p><ul><li>The XML Schema diagrams are transformed to the Class diagrams. Unnecessary stereotypes (XSDxxxx) are discarded from the classes.</li><li>Attributes of the classes are gathered if they were spread into several different classes.</li><li>Attributes of the classes may be realized as associations. In this case the main class gathers all the associations of the members.</li><li>The same principle is applied when elements are in a group shared by two or more classes. Elements (attributes) are copied into both destination classes.</li><li>The attributes with the «XSDgroupRef» stereotype are treated as if the group relationship has been drawn and transformed accordingly. They are discarded in the UML model, and the group content (elements / attributes) placed in their place.</li><li>Simple XML schema types (classes with the «XSDsimpleType» stereotype), which after copying and type remap are derived from any data type (UML DataType), or not derived from anything and are transformed into the UML data types.</li><li>Simple XML schema types derived by restriction from a string, restricted by enumerating string values, and are converted into enumerations in the UML diagrams.</li><li>The classes with the «XSDschema» stereotype are not copied into a destination model.</li><li>The «XDSkey», «XSDkeyref», and «XSDunique» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model.</li><li>The «XDSany», «XSDanyAttribute» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model. The «XDSnotation» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model.</li><li>The «XDSlength», «XDSminLength», «XDSmaxLength», «XSDpattern», «XSDfractionDigits», «XSDtotalDigits», «XDSmaxExclusive», «XDSmaxInclusive», «XDSminExclusive», and «XDSminInclusive» stereotyped attributes are not copied into a destination model.</li><li>The XML schemas (classes with the «XSDschema» stereotype) should not be transformed, but they may contain inner classes (anonymous types of schema elements). These inner classes are transformed using the usual rules for UML type transformation, as if they were not inner classes but normal XML schema types.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566817 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06823: XPDL Support

- page_id: `249566817`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566817/XPDL+Support
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:17.771+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1486920233

1486920233

1486920233

XML Process Definition Language (XPDL) is a serialization format for BPMN. XPDL provides a file format that supports all BPMN process definition description properties. It defines a description of both model element properties and graphical descriptions of the diagram. With XPDL, Cameo Business Modeler can export or import process definitions for or from other products to read. It also allows you to exchange your models with other tools to perform further model simulation, execution, or deployment.

Cameo Business Modeler plugin supports export of BPMN models to XPDL Version 2.2. This version is backward compatible with previous versions of XPDL and can be used to export BPMN2 and BPMN 1.x models.

Cameo Business Modeler supports importing from XPDL Versions 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2 to BPMN2. Business process models created with other tools (e.g. Visio, BizAgi, Process Architect, etc) can be imported to Cameo Business Modeler.

1486920232

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Exporting Models to XPDL

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fc232840-5793-469c-bac6-0fad68ce212e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486920233</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="37a586ad-93d3-4eb0-b2f0-deb56f613d71"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486920233</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01b4da1a-d53d-4a8a-bb67-fb7968836f63"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486920233</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>XML Process Definition Language (XPDL) is a serialization format for BPMN. XPDL provides a file format that supports all BPMN process definition description properties. It defines a description of both model element properties and graphical descriptions of the diagram. With XPDL, Cameo Business Modeler can export or import process definitions for or from other products to read. It also allows you to exchange your models with other tools to perform further model simulation, execution, or deployment.</p><p>Cameo Business Modeler plugin supports export of BPMN models to XPDL Version 2.2. This version is backward compatible with previous versions of XPDL and can be used to export BPMN2 and BPMN 1.x models.</p><p>Cameo Business Modeler supports importing from XPDL Versions 2.0, 2.1, and 2.2 to BPMN2. Business process models created with other tools (e.g. Visio, BizAgi, Process Architect, etc) can be imported to Cameo Business Modeler.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7a303aaf-e1c6-48dc-b3f3-da63d25fc375"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1486920232</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Exporting+Models+to+XPDL">Exporting Models to XPDL</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965797 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06824: Zoom tab

- page_id: `243965797`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965797/Zoom+tab
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:21:50.130+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface > Quick properties panel
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1425852029

1425852031

1425852030

INLINE

The **Zoom** tab enables you to preview a selected diagram. To select a diagram, go to the **Containment** or **Diagram** tab. You can also use the **Zoom** tab to zoom in or out of the active diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Zoom tab in the quick properties panel.

To open the **Zoom** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Zoom tab.
- On the Window menu, click Zoom .

To zoom in or out of the active diagram

- In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.

To quickly access any part of the diagram

- In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the diagram.

The selected diagram part will be displayed on the diagram pane.

To fit the diagram to the window

- In the Zoom tab, on the blue square [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_window_button.png'] , click the Fit in Window button or press CTRL+W .

To show the **Diagram Zoom Slider**

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment Options window opens.
2. Select the General options group > Display .
3. In the Display category, select the Show Diagram Zoom Slider check box.

The **Diagram Zoom Slider** is displayed in the **Zoom** tab (see the figure above).

1425852028

**Related pages**

- Model Browser

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a120925c-d0fc-4f76-9f93-cfc4353c396e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425852029</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="81270c1e-f695-48ce-aa7f-c00cf8e86585"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425852031</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b30ee70b-6ae1-4fdc-964a-c2a14f1fd7ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425852030</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8985cdc-1aa8-42fc-b4dc-133ca5d0ea02"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Zoom</strong> tab enables you to preview a selected diagram. To select a diagram, go to the <strong>Containment</strong> or <strong>Diagram</strong> tab. You can also use the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab to zoom in or out of the active diagram.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Zoom tab" ac:alt="Zoom tab"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_tab.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">The Zoom tab in the quick properties panel.</h6><p>To open the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Model Browser, click the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab.</li><li>On the <strong>Window</strong> menu, click <strong>Zoom</strong>.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To zoom in or out of the active diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To quickly access any part of the diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the diagram.</li></ul><p>The selected diagram part will be displayed on the diagram pane.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To fit the diagram to the window</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab, on the blue square <ac:image ac:height="28" ac:width="26"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fit_window_button.png" /></ac:image>, click the <strong>Fit in Window</strong> button or press <strong>CTRL+W</strong>.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To show the <strong>Diagram Zoom Slider</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> window opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>General</strong> options group &gt; <strong>Display</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Display</strong> category, select the <strong>Show Diagram Zoom Slider</strong> check box.</li></ol><p>The <strong>Diagram Zoom Slider</strong> is displayed in the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab (see the figure above).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3da31b57-b9b7-4760-87cf-4d8427074aa0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1425852028</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="ancestor-link parent-link"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Model+Browser">Model Browser</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967318 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06825: Zooming

- page_id: `243967318`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967318/Zooming
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:28.309+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

570200299

570200301

570200300

Zooming allows you to select a particular part of a diagram, zoom in, and make changes while working with a finer level of detail. You can also see an overview of a diagram by zooming out.

The **Diagram Zoom Slider**, located at the bottom right of the modeling tool, helps you with the zooming options. You can hide the slider if you do not need it.

To hide the Diagram Zoom Slider

1. In the main menu, click View > Status Line .
2. Click Show Diagram Zoom Slider to cancel the selection.

To fit the current diagram in the window, do one of the following

- In the main menu, click View > select Fit in Window (shortcut keys Ctrl+W).
- At the bottom right of the modeling tool, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_to_window_button.png'] Fit to Window icon.
- In the Model Browser > Zoom tab, click Fit to Window .

To zoom in on the current diagram, do one of the following

- In the main menu, click View and select Zoom In (shortcut keys Ctrl+NumPad + or scroll).
- At the bottom right of the modeling tool, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_in.png'] Zoom In button.

To zoom out from the current diagram, do one of the following

- In the main menu, click View and select Zoom Out (shortcut keys Ctrl+NumPad - or scroll).
- At the bottom right of the modeling tool, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_out.png'] Zoom Out button.

To restore the diagram to the original size, do one of the following

- In the main menu, click View and select Zoom 1:1 (shortcut keys Ctrl+NumPad /).
- At the bottom right of the modeling tool, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='zoom_11_button.png'] Zoom 1:1 button.

To view the selected shapes at maximum size

- Select the shapes, and from the View menu, select Zoom to Selection (shortcut keys Ctrl+NumPad *).

To select the zoom settings

- In the Environment Options dialog, Diagram options group, change the Zoom Step Size property. The maximum number is 1.0 (you can zoom a diagram twice.)

You can also zoom the diagram in or out using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Zoom tab' space=''] in the Model Browser.

570200298

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']
